Minimum of twenty (20) years of direct project management work experience working in high-rise construction. Previous experience working on complex and/ or high-risk projects. Knowledge & SkillsAdvanced knowledge of Quality Management, Risk Management practices and techniques, procurement, construction documents, contracts and contract law, Cost Control, Forecasting & Project Cost Reporting and Scheduling & Constructability. Demonstrated success in leading project teams to deliver projects on time and on budget. In-depth knowledge of the construction industry and company operations specifically construction specifications, building materials, methods and procedures. Strong conflict management skills and the ability to be persuasive and motivating. Understands consistent concerns and sees bigger picture across projects that impacts teams. Ability to lead multiple teams and to attract, identify and develop talent. Able to build and maintain lasting relationships with all business units. Strong Time Management for Multiple Teams/ ProjectsWorkforce PlanningLeadership Skills (building teams)Financial acumen & Cost analysis skillsConflict ManagementCoaching/ MentoringChange ManagementTeam DevelopmentAdditional InformationWhat we offer:Competitive Compensation PackageExtensive Health and Dental ProgramRRSP Program – Company MatchingRESP ProgramEmployee Family Assistance ProgramGenerous Vacation Day ProgramProfessional DevelopmentPersonal DaysEmployee Discount ProgramFun Company EventsCommunity InitiativesHealth and Wellness ProgramRecognition ProgramsLunch and LearnsAbout Us (Who is Clark Construction Management?):Clark Construction Management was established in 2017 under the leadership of Mike Clark and has continued to expand into a diverse and innovative team. We strive to be a leader in providing value-added construction services to our customers by creating a successful partnership with them throughout the construction process. Our ledge is to establish lasting relationships with our customers by exceeding their expectations and gaining their trust through exceptional performance by every member of the CCM team.Our Hiring Practices:Clark Construction Management's recruitment process includes accommodation for applicants with disabilities. Clark Construction Management is committed to accessibility for all, and we approach accessibility from an inclusive perspective and will do everything reasonable to accommodate.Clark Construction Management does not use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to screen, assess, or make decisions regarding candidates during the hiring process. Job postings may be distributed to third-party job platforms through our recruitment systems. While Clark Construction Management does not use AI directly, these platforms may use automated or AI-enabled features as part of their platform functionality; however, such features do not influence candidate screening or hiring decisions made by the company.This job posting reflects an existing vacancy.Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion:Clark Construction management is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive, and welcoming workplace. title: Project Manager
description: Position Title: Project ManagerLocation : Toronto ON Canada - Day 1 Onsite Project Planning & Delivery Management• Define and manage the end‑to‑end migration plan, including development, QA, non‑production and production readiness phases. Must be proactive in identifying tasks as the team is new to cloud migration.• Create intakes to engage resources (Consumers) and ensure that all teams submit estimates and identify their Dev and QA Leads.• Establish delivery milestones, timelines, and dependencies across development, testing, infrastructure, and deployment activities.• Coordinate phased delivery (e.g., development completion → QA → production rollout) to minimize risk and business disruption. Ability to manage the different phases in parallel is required.• Ensure delivery remains on schedule and within approved scope, escalating deviations as required. Stakeholder & Cross‑Functional Coordination• Serve as the central point of coordination across engineering, architecture, platform/cloud, security (SPIR), QA, operations (MOR), and business stakeholders.• Facilitate alignment between the Digital Core technical teams and business owners (Consumers) to ensure migrated services meet functional and enterprise expectations.• Maintain effective communication cadences and provide clear, executive‑level status reporting. Deliver Weekly status report to all stakeholders and Monthly report to Operating Committee. Governance, Risk & Dependency Management• Identify, monitor, and proactively manage project risks, with particular focus on migration‑specific risks such as:◦ Legacy system dependencies◦ Data integrity and service continuity◦ Integration with downstream consumers• Manage critical dependencies between on‑prem systems and cloud services to avoid downstream delays.• Ensure compliance with enterprise governance, security, privacy, and cloud standards. Cloud Migration Enablement• Support the transition from on‑prem infrastructure to cloud‑native services, ensuring teams understand migration scope, sequencing, and constraints.• Reinforce cloud‑first and reusable service principles, helping align delivery with long‑term modernization and cost‑reduction objectives.• Coordinate infrastructure readiness (e.g., environments, access, tooling) required to support development, QA, and production deployment. Quality & Readiness Oversight• Ensure clear entry and exit criteria between phases (development, QA, production).• Coordinate QA readiness, including test planning, defect/variance triage processes, and deployment readiness reviews.• Validate that documentation, support models, and operational handoffs are in place ahead of production cutover. Financial & Value Management• Track project progress against approved budget and resourcing assumptions.• Ensure project properties in clarity is complete, accurate and up to date.• Manage the team list, forecasts run cost plans first week of each month.• Provide justification/rationale for any cost variance between forecast and actual.• Run PM Audit Attestation. Executive Reporting & Decision Support• Provide clear, concise updates to sponsors and leadership on progress, risks, and key decisions – Monthly.• Provide status updates to the project team and consumers – Weekly.• Prepare leadership for upcoming milestones (e.g., QA start, production deployment).• Enable timely decisions through transparent data, options, and impact assessments.

title: Assistant Project Manager
description: Who We Are Rising Edge Group is an electrical contracting company founded in 2002, specialization in high-voltage electrical solutions that power critical infrastructure across North America. With operations in both Canada and the United States, we partner with clients across the electrical utilities, renewable energy, and industrial sectors to design, build, and maintain the critical infrastructure systems communities and industries rely on.  Driven by a clear vision to strengthen the stability, reliability, and advancement of energy infrastructure, we take pride in delivering work that matters. Guided by our STRIDE values - Safety, Teamwork, Responsibility, Integrity, Deliver, and Excellence - we foster a people focused on a culture of growth, mentorship and collaboration where safety always come first. What You'll Do Reporting to the Program Manager, the Assistant Project Manager is responsible for working with an assigned Project Manager(s) to coordinate and accomplish the necessary project tasks and requirements. The role requires the individual to closely communicate with the Project Manager(s) to ensure key milestones are proactively being monitored and executed on all assigned projects.Key ResponsibilitiesThe responsibilities include, but may not be limited to: Support project set-up following award and estimate handoff by ensuring project team structure, budget alignment, documentation, and systems are established for an effective kick-offContribute to the development and execution of the project execution plan, including critical path analysis, risk identification, resource planning, and milestone scheduling to ensure on-time milestone achievementsAdminister project changes in accordance with established change management procedures, ensuring accurate documentation and clear communication to the Client and project teamMaintain comprehensive project documentation, including the project execution plan, risk register, master schedule, health and safety plan, quality plan, and all required Rising Edge documentationMonitor project expenditures and provide regular cost reporting to the Project Manager while tracking budget performance, forecast costs, and align man-hour projections within approved budgetsCoordinate procurement activities, including materials, equipment, and subcontracted services, to ensure timely and successful project deliverySupport cash flow management by ensuring timely processing of accounts receivable and payable invoicesPrepare and maintain internal and external project reporting while facilitating clear and effective communication between the Client, project team, and key stakeholdersUnder the direction of the Project Manager(s), support project meetings including kick-offs, progress reviews, change order discussions, recovery planning, and lessons learned sessionsMonitor and report on project safety, quality and environmental compliance and acceptance requirementsAssist in identifying project staffing needs, including specialty consultants and subcontractors and support the establishment of clear roles, responsibilities, and performance expectationsSupport project close-out activities and post-project reviews to identify performance gaps and continuous improvement opportunitiesPerform additional duties as assigned by the Project Manager(s) to support the safe, efficient, and successful execution of assigned projectsAdditional Information   As a condition of employment, candidates will be required to complete pre-employment screening including professional reference checks and a criminal record check.RequirementsWhat You'll BringUndergraduate degree in Electrical Engineering, Business, Technologist or a related field requiredProject Management Professional (PMP) designation or equivalent is preferredAt least 1 - 3 years' experience managing medium to large full cycle construction projects requiredExcellent problem solving and analytical skills and a demonstrated ability to manage a diverse team in a dynamic, dead-line oriented environment are an assetProficiency in MS Project, Primavera P6, HardDollar (InEight) and ViewPoint Construction Software preferredValid class 5 driver's license with an acceptable driving record required  BenefitsWhat We Offer Competitive compensation with weekly pay periods Generous paid time off   RRSP-DPSP employer matching, increasing with years of service  Comprehensive benefits package (medical, dental, vision, and disability)  Ongoing training, development, and career advancement opportunities Employee Assistance Program (confidential counselling support) Employee Referral Bonus Program  Why Rising Edge Group?  Safety & Excellence: Safety is at the heart of everything we do. Every project is approached with diligence, strategic planning, and reliable execution practices to protect our teams and the clients we serve while delivering world-class high-voltage solutions. Our unwavering commitment to safety and quality drives excellence on every project, every day.  People-First Culture: You're more than a job title. Every person contributes to powering essential energy infrastructure across North America. Our teams are built on collaboration, respect, and accountability. We support our employees through meaningful engagement initiatives, team events, and a culture that prioritizes wellbeing, inclusion, and genuine lasting connections.Career Growth & Impact: Your role is a path to a long-term career. We empower our employees with hands-on experience, exposure to diverse projects, and opportunities to advance across disciplines, locations, and career paths. Through continuous learning, mentorship, and professional development, you'll build lasting expertise while making a tangible impact in an environment where teamwork and integrity guide our work.Reward & Recognition: We believe skill, dedication, and excellence deserves recognition. Competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits, and a strong culture of appreciation ensure your achievements are celebrated, your growth is supported, and your work truly makes a difference.  At Rising Edge Group, you're not just building projects, you're building a meaningful career where your work helps power the future, one project at a time.

title: Junior Project Manager & Quality Assurance Specialist
description: The Klick Group—comprising Klick Health (including Klick Katalyst and btwelve), Klick Media Group, Klick Applied Sciences (including Klick Labs), Klick Consulting, and Klick Ventures—is an ecosystem dedicated to realizing the full potential of our people and clients in life sciences. Founded in 1997, we have offices in New York, Philadelphia, Saratoga Springs, Toronto, London, São Paulo, and Singapore. Consistently named a Best Managed Company, Most Admired Corporate Culture, and Great Place to Work, Klick is committed to fostering a high-performance, people-first culture with creativity, collaboration, innovation, and impact across everything we do.About Our Accounting TeamThe Accounting team supports Klick’s financial operations by ensuring accuracy, compliance, and reliability across systems and processes. The team partners closely with Technology, Operations, and business stakeholders to improve internal tools and workflows. Their work enables informed decision-making and operational efficiency. The team values collaboration, clarity, and continuous improvement.Job DescriptionThe Junior Project Manager & Quality Assurance Specialist supports the delivery and quality of internal tools and systems used by the Accounting team. This role helps coordinate project activities, supports Agile workflows, and ensures solutions are thoroughly tested before release. The position offers exposure to the full project lifecycle, from early planning through deployment and ongoing improvement, in a collaborative, learning-focused environment.What You’ll DoCoordinate project tasks, timelines, and dependencies with Accounting partners, developers, and stakeholders using Agile delivery practicesExecute manual quality assurance testing on internal tools and applications, documenting defects, edge cases, and improvement opportunitiesSupport Agile ceremonies by tracking daily priorities, upcoming work, and progress across sprintsMaintain clear project and QA documentation, including requirements, test results, and status updates, in shared toolsContribute to post-launch maintenance and iterative improvements to Accounting systems and workflowsQualificationsRequired:3+ years of experience supporting digital projects, software initiatives, or internal tools, including coordination or delivery supportHands-on experience performing manual QA testing for web-based or internal applications, with documented test cases or defect trackingFamiliarity with Agile or Scrum workflows, including stand-ups, sprint planning, or retrospectives, in a team-based environmentExperience organizing project documentation, requirements, or testing results in shared collaboration toolsAbility to communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders when identifying issues and discussing solutionsDesired:Comfort and enthusiasm for using AI as a regular part of how work gets doneFormal Agile or Scrum training, coursework, or certificationInterest in how technology and process improvements can enhance finance or accounting operationsExperience working in cross-functional teams supporting internal business usersExposure to issue tracking or project management tools such as Jira, Confluence, or similar platformsAdditional InformationThis posting is for a newly created role at Klick.The base salary range for this position is between CAD $75,000 - $80,000. The final amount offered will be determined based on several factors such as a candidate's work location, their unique skill set, education, and prior work experience.Our Commitment to InclusionKlick uses an AI-powered tool as part of an automated process to support our hiring process by analyzing your experience, skills, and qualifications to help identify alignment with role requirements. Please note that the tool does not make final decisions or automatically disqualify candidates. Hiring decisions are made by our team, with thoughtful human judgment and oversight. We are committed to fair and transparent hiring practices and have implemented safeguards to help mitigate bias and support compliance with applicable laws. If you have questions, accessibility needs, or concerns about the use of this tool, please contact us. Klick is consciously creating a culture where everyone can thrive and grow in their careers. We believe that our best work comes from our diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. We strongly encourage members of historically underrepresented communities to apply, including Black people, Indigenous peoples, and other people of color, people with disabilities, people from gender and sexually diverse communities and people with intersectional identities. We're also committed to developing an inclusive, barrier-free recruitment process and work environment. Should you require any accommodation, please contact us at careers@klick.com and we will work with you to meet your accessibility needs and ensure you have a positive experience.

title: Project Manager
description: Inclusion without Exception:Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an equal opportunity employer, and embraces diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability, neurodiversity, and sexual orientation, to create a workforce that reflects the societies we operate in. Our continued commitment to Culture and Diversity is reflected in our people stories across our workforce and implemented through equitable workplace policies and processes.About TCS:TCS is an IT services, consulting, and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys for over 55 years. Its consulting-led, cognitive-powered portfolio of business, technology, and engineering services and solutions is delivered through its unique Location Independent Agile delivery model, recognized as a benchmark of excellence in software development. A part of the Tata group, India's largest multinational business group, TCS operates in 55 countries and employs over 607,000 highly skilled individuals, including more than 10,000 in Canada. The company generated consolidated revenues of US $ 30 billion in the fiscal year ended March 31, 2025, and is listed on the BSE and the NSE in India. TCS' proactive stance on climate change and award-winning work with communities across the world have earned it a place in leading sustainability indices such as the MSCI Global Sustainability Index and the FTSE4Good Emerging Index.Required Skill Sets:• Lead project planning, scheduling, tracking, and execution.• Manage project scope, timelines, risks, and dependencies.• Coordinate with cross functional teams to ensure timely delivery.• Prepare project dashboards, status reports, and executive updates.• Facilitate requirement gathering, sprint planning, and review meetings.• Ensure adherence to governance, quality standards, and organizational processes.• Manage stakeholder expectations and resolve project roadblocks proactively.• Drive continuous improvement and operational excellence within the project teamSalary Range - CA$ 90,000 - CA$ 120,000 Per YearNote: • TCS does not use artificial intelligence tools for candidate screening or evaluation.• This posting is for a current vacancy• The hiring process includes an initial screening by the TCS Hiring Team, followed by a technical evaluation and managerial discussion conducted by the Business Team, and concluding with the final HR evaluation.Tata Consultancy Services Canada Inc. is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) and the Ontario Human Rights Code (OHRC). Should you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please inform Human Resources.Thank you for your interest in TCS. Candidates that meet the qualifications for this position will be contacted within a 2-week period. We invite you to continue to apply for other opportunities that match your profile.

title: Integrated Studio Project Manager

built a strong agency foundation and is ready to independently run integrated production workstreams, while continuing to grow toward senior-level Studio leadership.You will manage production timelines, budgets, workflows, and cross-functional collaboration between Agency and Studio production teams.What You’ll Be Responsible For:Manage the production of creative assets required to support initiativesIdentify risks and recommend proactive solutionsWhat You Bring:4–7 years of project management experience within an advertising agency (required)Experience managing integrated campaigns collaborationExperience managing budgets and scopesAutomotive experience (asset)Post-secondary education in Business, Marketing, or related field (asset)What Sets You Apart:Strong communicator with title: Project Manager (Business Solutions/Technology)

TorontoResponsibilitiesManage the planning and delivery of technology projects across the Business Solutions & Technology team, ensuring initiatives meet timelines and objectives.Develop and maintain project plans, schedules, and task tracking to support efficient project execution.Coordinate activities across IT, Business Intelligence, Digital Solutions, and other internal teams to ensure alignment across workstreams.Identify and manage project risks, dependencies, and resource requirements, supporting mitigation and resolution efforts.Track and coordinate ongoing operational workstreams including system enhancements, reporting requests, digital platform updates, and infrastructure initiatives.Support the intake, triage, and prioritization of technology requests from internal stakeholders, particularly fundraising and donor engagement teams.Maintain visibility into operational workloads and support team leads in balancing operational support with project delivery.Support the implementation and continuous improvement of project governance, delivery processes, and portfolio tracking.Maintain structured workflows and project tracking using tools such as Asana, Jira, or similar platforms.Facilitate collaboration between technology teams and business stakeholders to ensure alignment between technical delivery and organizational needs.Coordinate with external vendors and partners supporting CRM systems, data integrations, and digital platforms.Maintain project documentation including project plans, timelines, status reports, and risk logs.Provide regular project updates and portfolio reporting to leadership to ensure visibility into progress and priorities.Requirements5–7+ years of project management experience, preferably within technology, digital platforms, or data environments.Demonstrated experience managing multiple concurrent projects and operational workstreams.Familiarity with work management tools such as Asana, Jira, or similar platforms.Working understanding of enterprise technology environments including CRM systems, digital platforms, infrastructure, and data/reporting tools.Strong organizational and coordination skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and cross-functional coordination skills.Experience working with external vendors and managing partner deliverables.Project management certifications such as PMP, Agile, or similar are considered an asset.Experience working in nonprofit or mission-driven organizations is considered an asset.

title: Project Manager
description: Who We AreWelcome to TELUS Digital — where innovation drives impact at a global scale. As an award-winning digital product consultancy and the digital division of TELUS, one of Canada’s largest telecommunications providers, we design and deliver transformative customer experiences through cutting-edge technology, agile thinking, and a people-first culture.With a global team across North America, South America, Central America, Europe, Africa, and APAC, we offer end-to-end expertise across eight core service areas: Digital Product Consulting, Digital Marketing Services, Data & AI, Strategy Consulting, Business Operations Modernization, Enterprise Applications, Cloud Engineering, and QA & Test Engineering.From mobile apps and websites to voice UI, chatbots, AI, customer service, and in-store solutions, TELUS Digital enables seamless, trusted, and digitally powered experiences that meet customers wherever they are — all backed by the secure infrastructure and scale of our multi-billion-dollar parent company.The Opportunity We are looking for a highly organized, proactive intermediate Project Manager to join our team as a Full-Time Employee (FTE). This is a 100% remote role designed for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments and possesses a blend of technical curiosity and operational discipline.The ideal candidate acts as the "glue" between technical execution and stakeholder expectations, ensuring that complex AI and Cloud initiatives move from ideation to go-live without a hitch.ResponsibilitiesEnd-to-End Coordination: Support technical project delivery across the full lifecycle, managing timelines, dependencies, and parallel workstreams.Multi-Project Management: Simultaneously track multiple projects, maintaining clear priorities and a rigorous delivery discipline.Stakeholder Communication: Bridge the gap between technical teams and non-technical business units with structured updates and clear reporting.Risk & Issue Management: Proactively identify potential bottlenecks; anticipate and raise risks early to ensure project health.Documentation & Reporting: Prepare comprehensive status reports, including progress tracking and risk logs, using tools like Jira and Confluence.Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work within Agile or hybrid environments to facilitate seamless handoffs between data, AI, and cloud teams.QualificationsExperience: 2–5 years of hands-on project management or coordination experience, specifically within a tech-focused environment.Technical Literacy: Exposure to AI, Data, or Cloud-based projects. Familiarity with GCP (Google Cloud Platform) is a significant advantage.Methodology: Working knowledge of Agile and/or hybrid delivery frameworks.Tooling: Proficiency in Jira, Asana, Azure DevOps, Confluence, Google Workspace, and advanced Excel.Mindset: A "take-initiative" attitude. You don’t just track tasks; you own the outcome and anticipate the next three steps.Equal Opportunity EmployerAt TELUS Digital, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. All aspects of employment, including the decision to hire and promote, are based on applicants’ qualifications, merits, competence and performance without regard to any characteristic related to diversity.We will only use the information you provide to process your application and to produce tracking statistics. Since we do not request personal data deemed sensitive, we ask you to abstain from sharing that information with us.For more information on how we use your information, see our Privacy Policy.Compensation Range: CA$90K - CA$120K

title: Sr. Project Manager (6 month contract)
description: At PointClickCare our mission is simple: to help providers deliver exceptional care. And that starts with our people. As a leading health tech company that’s founder-led and privately held, we empower our employees to push boundaries, innovate, and shape the future of healthcare.With the largest long-term and post-acute care dataset and a Marketplace of 400+ integrated partners, our platform serves over 30,000 provider organizations, making a real difference in millions of lives. We also reinvest a significant percentage of our revenue back into research and development, ensuring our employees have the resources to innovate and make a lasting impact. Recognized by Forbes as a top private cloud company and honored as one of Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures, we offer flexibility, growth opportunities, and meaningful work.At PointClickCare, we empower our people to be the architects of a smarter healthcare future; one that is human-first and accelerated by AI to create meaningful and lasting change. Employees harness AI as a catalyst for creativity, productivity, and thoughtful decision-making. By integrating AI tools into our daily workflows, collaboration is enhanced, outcomes are improved, and every team member has the proficiency to maximize their impact. It all starts with our hiring practices where we uncover AI expertise that complements our mission, and we continue to invest in training and development to nurture innovation throughout the employee journey.Join us in redefining healthcare — so it doesn’t just survive, it thrives. To learn more about PointClickCare, check out Life at PointClickCare and connect with us on Glassdoor and LinkedIn.Position SummaryReporting to the Director, Portfolio Management, you will act as the primary client liaison to lead the implementation of projects and program workstreams within the Strategic Delivery Office of PointClickCare. You will support multi-year enterprise transformation programs and/or individual projects governed by the SDO, ensuring alignment with transformation strategy and portfolio-level objectives. This role will be responsible for managing both standalone projects and workstreams embedded within larger transformation programs, requiring strong collaboration with Program Directors, Business stakeholders, OCM partners, and Enterprise Architecture teams.Key ResponsibilitiesResponsible for using best practice PMO methodology to create a project plan to fit the stakeholders/customers’ needs and deliver within budget on desired outcomes and ensure alignment with SDO governance, intake, and prioritization standards.Leads the project with clear ownership, ensuring alignment, momentum, and cohesion across all workstreams.Working closely with different business units to execute project scope and objectives while ensuring alignment with broader transformation program roadmaps, sequencing, and dependencies. Participate in integrated planning rhythms across SDO programs.Manage the project budget, scope, team resources, communications, and project plans, ensuring scope and solutions are aligned to expected business outcomes to support benefits realization.You will proactively integrate Change Management into project planning, collaborating with Program Change Directors to manage readiness, impacts, communications, adoption needs, and collision risks across the portfolio.Organize, lead, facilitate meetings with clear objectives, compile timely meeting summary minutes.Reports and effectively communicates to executive sponsors and business stakeholders: project milestones, budget tracking, status updates, as well as any existing or potential escalation issues. Proactively follow escalation and change control processes. Has overall ownership of all management reports on a given project. Reporting must follow SDO standard formats, contributing to portfolio dashboards and integrated program reporting.Leverage data-driven metrics and AI-enabled insights to support forecasting, risk identification, and stakeholder communication.Risk and Issue management. Have the knowledge, skills, and experience to recognize when problems surface or potential problems are looming. You can articulate problems, bring the right people together to solve problems and know when the problem has been properly addressed and closed, all this with the proper sense of urgency that the problem requires.Drives ownership of decisions to the level where the accountability of the decision must lie.Coordinate with Vendors, as necessary, from SOW, onboarding into project team and holding Vendor deliverables accountable to schedule and per contract. Manage Vendor relationships and lead calls with vendors.Thoroughly understanding our business functions and technical needs.Managing the day-to-day implementation of operational project aspects, defining project tasks and resource allocation.Foster continued growth of the team’s project management capabilities, improving knowledge and experience within the PMO.Contribute to the overall goals and objectives of the Project Management Office.Required Skills & Experience5-10 years’ experience in project management3-5 years' experience in SaaS business environmentsExperience in managing at least one of the following types of projects: Enterprise-wide transformation programs, Technology modernization projects, Multi-vendor delivery projects, or Cross-functional multi-team delivery initiatives.Experience delivering workstreams within enterprise-wide transformation programs (multi-year, cross-functional).Experience working within matrixed governance environments with cross-program dependencies.Experience in managing enterprise information management programs, corporates data office projects or digital transformation projects.PMP or Agile designation preferred.Experience partnering with OCM teams to manage change impacts and readiness.Self-motivated, strong and demonstrated business acumen, assertive.Extremely detailed oriented, organized, exceptional time management skills.Excellent communication, time management, scope management with the ability to interpret portfolio dashboards, metrics, and data insights to drive decisions.Comfortable working with AI-assisted delivery and analysis tools.Excellent knowledge of project metrics, including gathering, reporting, trend analysis, creation, and metrics.Ability to identify issues and problems, generate solutions and choose appropriate alternatives using basic root cause analysis.Manage project scope by assessing requirements changes, determining, and conveying risks and/or impacts to budget, schedule and resourcing.Proficient in MS Office applications, Aha!, JIRA or other project management tools.At PointClickCare, base salary is one of the many components that make up our total rewards package. The Canada base hourly range for this position is $52.88 - $58.65 ($110,000 - $122,000 annual),non-overtime eligible. Our salary ranges are determined by job and level. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the target for new hire salaries for the position across all Canada locations. Within the range, individual compensation is determined by job-related skills and knowledge, relevant experience including professional and lived experience, and/or work location. Your recruiter can share more information about our total rewards package during the hiring process.

title: Senior Project Manager
description: It’s an exciting time to join MGAC Canada!MGAC is a global boutique consultancy dedicated to managing and representing our clients’ best interests. Our success is rooted in hiring exceptional people who are driven to do challenging work with the unique blend of experience, integrity, creativity, and commitment that separates us from our competitors.We have three simple goals: to do the most interesting and challenging work, to have fun working together, and to build a successful business in the process.Our Toronto office is in Toronto’s Junction neighborhood at Toronto 213 Sterling Road, near the Bloor Subway line, UP Express, Bloor Go Line and both the College and Dundas Streetcars. Our walking score of 100 helps our commitment to our ESG plan!This position requires you to be in the Toronto office or project site 4-5 days per week with the possibility to work from home as needed.What You’ll Be DoingMGAC’s Canada team manages the construction of large multi sector projects with deep industry expertise. We act as owner’s representatives—resolving issues, managing conflicts, and guiding informed decisions to keep projects on track. As a Project Manager, with a strong focus on implementing robust project controls, you will lead incredible, resume-building, high-visibility projectsUnlock Your PotentialWe’re Looking for candidates eager to take ownership of challenging projects and grow their careers. Here’s what will set you up for success at MGAC;A proactive, problem-solving and analytical mindset.Bachelor’s Degree, or equivalent experience in related discipline, Architecture, Engineering and/or Construction.OAA or P.Eng LicensePMP Certification15+ years project management experience in all phases of the project lifecycle pre-construction, construction, commissioning, close out, operation readiness5+ years of experience managing critical environment construction projects (e.g., data centers, labs, hospitals)MEP experience in ground-up construction.Strong project controls skills within design management, schedule, cost, risk, contract administration, claims avoidance, stakeholder management, and compliance management.Strong knowledge of construction contracts, CCDC, P3, Progressive, Alliance etc.Demonstrated experience in the ability to determine corrective actions or recommendations based on facts and figures and critically evaluating information and data from various sources.Excellent analytical, organizational, problem-solving, presentation, interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills.Ambitious, hardworking and project management-focused, and seeking a modern and energetic business that will empower you to grow your career.Agile skills that can be applied to solving the problem at hand.Excited to work on complex and challenging projects.Day To Day ResponsibilitiesPlan and manage complex capital projects within the Healthcare, Education and Critical Environments sectors.Lead project planning and execution: define scope, objectives, milestones, and deliverables; develop and maintain the project schedule; establish robust governance and decision-making processes.Stakeholder management: identify, engage, and manage expectations of clients, owners, design teams, subcontractors, suppliers, authorities having jurisdiction, and internal sponsors; maintain proactive communication.Budgeting and financial control: develop and manage the project budget; monitor costs, forecast cash flow, manage change orders, track earned value where used, and ensure financial targets are met.Risk, safety, and quality management: identify and manage project risks; implement risk mitigation plans; lead safety leadership and ensure adherence to quality standards and regulatory requirements.Contract and procurement management: oversee contracting strategy, procurement scheduling, bid evaluation, subcontractor selection, and contract administration; manage changes, claims, and closeout activities.Schedule and performance management: own the master schedule; monitor progress, critical path, and look-ahead planning; drive schedule recovery plans when needed.Quality and compliance: ensure work complies with design intent, codes, standards, permits, and client requirements; lead QA/QC activities and audits.Team leadership and development: build and manage project teams; assign responsibilities; mentor, coach, and evaluate performance; foster a collaborative, high-performance culture.Stakeholder reporting and governance: prepare and present progress reports, dashboards, and milestone reviews; maintain documentation for auditability and governance compliance.Change management: assess, approve, and manage scope changes; analyze impact on schedule, cost, risk, and quality; coordinate with client and design teams.Commissioning and handover: plan and supervise commissioning, testing, startup activities, and final handover to client; ensure as-built documentation and warranties are completed.Design coordination: oversee design development, reviews, clashes, and integration with construction sequencing; manage RFI processes and design clarifications.Trade and interface management: coordinate with multiple trades (carried by the owner/client); anticipate and resolve interdependencies; manage interfaces between disciplines.Permitting and approvals: navigate regulatory approvals, permits, inspections, and approvals required for project progression.Logistics and site management: understand plan site access, material handling, crane/rigging plans, and on-site workflow to optimize productivity and safety.Commissioning planning: ensure readiness for client acceptance and warranties.Client relationship and value delivery: understand client objectives, align project outcomes to business value, and identify opportunities for value realization.Portfolio and program alignment (where applicable): ensure project aligns with broader program goals, standards, and methodologies; participate in PMO governance.Change leadership and culture: champion continuous improvement, lean practices where applicable, and foster resilience within the team.Data-driven decision making: base actions on accurate data, metrics, and trend analysis; use dashboards and KPIs to guide decisions.Scheduling: Primavera P6 or MS Project; critical path analysis; look-ahead planning; baselining and change control.Cost management: budgets, forecasts, cost-to-complete, earned value (where used).Risk management: risk registers, mitigation plans, risk workshops, and issue logs.Documentation: project plans, procurement schedules, RFI/PO/CO processes, change orders, meeting minutes, and closeout documentation.Reporting: weekly, bi-weekly, and monthly progress reports; client and internal steering committee updates; dashboards and KPI reviews.Why Work With Us?28+ years strong in North America and growing fast—with a positive, energizing culture.Consistently recognized as a Top Workplace with a true employee-first mindset.Entrepreneurial spirit encouraged—bring fresh ideas and exceed client expectations.Global reach, yet agile and client-focused like a boutique firm.Your voice matters—flat structure, open doors, and recognition for your contributions.Strong work-life balance, generous PTO, and top-tier wellness benefits.Our Canadian project work is expanding, with a healthy pipeline and numerous opportunities on the horizon.Beyond the Paycheck: Discover Our Benefits Vacation starts on day one—accrue 3 weeks per year, plus a paid birthday off.Competitive benefits that support both personal and professional growth.Support continuous Professional Development.

Facilitate communication across departments to ensure seamless integration of project activities.Compliance And Standards Ensure all project activities comply with relevant laws, regulations, and company policies.QualificationsUndergraduate degree.Minimum 1-3 years of project management experience, including leadership experience and the ability to manage complex projects with advanced knowledge of project management methodologies and best practices.Candidates with previous Emergency Management Response experience will be prioritized.Project Management Professional certification not required but is an asset.Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, capable of effectively presenting project updates and strategic information to stakeholders and senior management.Proficient in negotiation techniques, capable of managing client expectations and reaching mutually beneficial solutions. Experience in resolving conflicts and negotiating project terms with stakeholders to align with organizational objectives.Strong understanding of quality assurance processes and methodologies. Ability to implement QA protocols to ensure project deliverables meet or exceed the established standards. Skilled in identifying, addressing, and mitigating quality risks throughout the project lifecycle.Ability to deliver presentations to a diverse audience, including senior executives, clients, and project teams. Proficient in utilizing various presentation tools and techniques to effectively communicate complex ideas and project progress.Demonstrated leadership skills with the ability to mentor, motivate, and direct cross-functional teams. Committed to fostering a culture of high performance and continuous improvement.This is a Calgary office-based position.Other InformationH2S offers Generous paid time off, comprehensive health care plan, company matching RRSP program, annual performance bonus, professional development subsidies, employee referral incentive. Additional InformationSGS is an Equal Opportunity Employer, and as such we recruit, hire, train, and promote persons in all job classifications without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability, age, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression and Indigenous status, or any other characteristics protected by law.To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily with or without reasonable accommodations. description: Project Manager, Commercial Canada Division: CommercialJob location: Remote (Ontario, Quebec, Alberta)Hours: 40 hours per week, Monday to FridayEmployment type: Full TimeA little about usHi, we’re Aurora Cannabis, and we’re so excited to meet you!Aurora Cannabis is a leader in the rapidly evolving global cannabis industry, serving both medical and consumer markets worldwide. Dedicated to helping people improve their lives, Aurora is driven by science and innovation, with a focus on providing premium medical cannabis products produced with the highest standards. We proudly serve both patients and consumers across Canada, Europe, Australia and New Zealand. Our global brand portfolio includes Aurora, Being, CanniMed, CraftPlant, Daily Special, Drift, Greybeard, IndiMed, MedReleaf, Pedanios, San Raf, Tasty’s, Whistler Cannabis Co, and WMMC. Aurora's brands continue to break through as industry leaders in the medical, wellness and adult recreational markets wherever they are launched.At Aurora, employees are at the heart of everything we do as we execute with excellence and agility. Whether in our manufacturing facilities, in our state-of-the-art research labs or supporting our global business development, or, within our corporate functions such as HR, operations, government affairs, and more – our diverse A-Team leads with expertise, passion and dedication every day. Together, we are opening the world to cannabis and making a meaningful impact for patients, consumers, our communities and our A-team.Job SummaryThe Project Manager, Commercial Canada acts as a strategic execution partner to the Commercial leadership team, responsible for planning, coordinating, and delivering key commercial initiatives and client implementation projects across a complex, matrix organization. Serving as a bridge between strategy and execution, this role drives alignment across cross-functional stakeholders, manages critical workstreams, and ensures commercial priorities are executed with speed, agility, and discipline.The ideal candidate brings strong project management rigor and commercial acumen, with the ability to manage executive-level stakeholders, navigate matrixed teams, and implement structured reporting and governance. This role supports leadership through clear communication, performance tracking, and proactive issue resolution, ensuring initiatives are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned to business growth and profitability objectives. Interesting? Here’s a little more…As the Project Manager, Commercial Canada you will be involved in …Project Management & Execution Lead the planning and execution of commercial projects including new product launches, trade programs, promotional campaigns, and key account initiatives. Develop detailed project plans, timelines, and progress reports to ensure visibility and accountability across functions. Coordinate cross-functional teams (Sales, Marketing, Channel Partnerships, E-Commerce) to ensure timely delivery of project components. Track performance against key milestones, budgets, and KPIs, and proactively address risks or barriers to success.Sales Enablement & Support Collaborate with Sales and Marketing teams to implement go-to-market strategies and ensure sales teams are equipped for launch readiness. Support sales teams in preparing client proposals, RFP submissions, and presentations that align with business goals and compliance standards. Oversee pricing approvals, contract administration, and operational components tied to commercial execution.Commercial Strategy & Executive Reporting Partner with Commercial leadership to develop board decks, quarterly business reviews, and strategy presentations that highlight performance, pipeline, and growth opportunities. title: Project Manager
description: Résumé du poste: Le poste de gestionnaire de projet joue un rôle clé chez TransPerfect Translations. Les gestionnaires de projet exécutent tous les projets du début à la fin et constituent l’unique point de responsabilité quant à leur réussite. Le gestionnaire de projet est responsable de l’ensemble du cycle de vie de tous les projets qui lui sont confiés et sert de point de contact et de responsabilité unique.Description:• Confirmer la clarté des instructions avant de commencer le flux de travail du projet, en assurant la liaison avec le chargé de compte pour modifier/corriger les instructions/paramètres du projet, au besoin.• Coordonner avec les chargés de compte afin d’informer et de conseiller les clients sur la meilleure façon d’atteindre leurs objectifs pour des projets individuels, en communiquant potentiellement directement avec les clients.• Négocier les tarifs et les échéances avec les linguistes contractuels afin de maximiser la rentabilité et la rapidité d’exécution du projet.• Utiliser les outils de traduction assistée par ordinateur dans la mesure du possible pour maximiser la cohérence et la rentabilité.• Utiliser les documents de référence/glossaires appropriés au besoin pour maximiser la cohérence et la qualité.• Communiquer immédiatement tout problème, retard ou renseignement supplémentaire au chargé de compte et tenter de résoudre tout problème qui pourrait survenir.• Assurer efficacement la liaison avec les autres services qui sont des parties essentielles du flux de travail du projet (par exemple, les gestionnaires de la qualité, l’équipe de publication assistée par ordinateur, etc.).• Effectuer une vérification finale visuelle de la qualité et émettre une cote de qualité pour le gestionnaire de la qualité avant d’envoyer une tâche.• Retourner le projet terminé au chargé de compte à temps ou même à l’avance.• Facturer les projets de manière complète et précise dans les 24 heures suivant l’achèvement du projet.• Établir et entretenir des relations de travail avec des linguistes contractuels du monde entier : fournir régulièrement des commentaires positifs et négatifs; classer les formulaires de mesures correctives au besoin pour le travail non conforme effectué et travailler avec l’assurance qualité pour imposer des sanctions financières appropriées dans ces cas.• Examiner et résoudre les plaintes des clients, résoudre les problèmes et travailler avec l’assurance qualité/la direction pour s’assurer que les changements systémiques appropriés sont mis en œuvre afin de s’assurer que tout problème qui s’est produit ne peut pas être répété.• En fonction de l’approbation de la direction, participer à la formation des nouveaux employés/chargés de compte, au besoin.• Sur la base de l’approbation de la direction, assister à des salons professionnels/conférences/réunions avec les clients, au besoin, et/ou exécuter des exemples de travaux et/ou de projets avec un niveau plus élevé de complexité/difficulté.• Travailler à comprendre et à connaître vos clients ainsi que votre marché vertical, y compris les événements actuels, les tendances commerciales et les chefs de file de l’industrie.• Faire des recommandations à votre gestionnaire sur les façons d’améliorer tout processus, etc. dans le cadre de l’engagement de TransPerfect envers l’amélioration continue.• Exécute d’autres projets ou tâches spéciales au besoin.Compétences requises:• Excellentes aptitudes de communication écrite et orale en anglais et en français.• Minimum d’un baccalauréat ou l’équivalent.• Capacité de mener efficacement plusieurs tâches à la fois afin d’exécuter simultanément plusieurs projets.• Détenir des aptitudes exceptionnelles en résolution de problèmes et en pensée critique.• Doit faire preuve de professionnalisme en toute situation, en particulier lors d'échéances très restreintes.• Résultats satisfaisants aux tests de l’entreprise.Compétences et expérience valorisées, mais non requises :• Une expérience title: Assistant Project Manager
description: Who We Are Rising Edge Group is an electrical contracting company founded in 2002, specialization in high-voltage electrical solutions that power critical infrastructure across North America. With operations in both Canada and the United States, we partner with clients across the electrical utilities, renewable energy, and industrial sectors to design, build, and maintain the critical infrastructure systems communities and industries rely on.  Driven by a clear vision to strengthen the stability, reliability, and advancement of energy infrastructure, we take pride in delivering work that matters. Guided by our STRIDE values - Safety, Teamwork, Responsibility, Integrity, Deliver, and Excellence - we foster a people focused on a culture of growth, mentorship and collaboration where safety always come first. What You'll Do Reporting to the Program Manager, the Assistant Project Manager is responsible for working with an assigned Project Manager(s) to coordinate and accomplish the necessary project tasks and requirements. The role requires the individual to closely communicate with the Project Manager(s) to ensure key milestones are proactively being monitored and executed on all assigned projects.Key ResponsibilitiesThe responsibilities include, but may not be limited to: Support project set-up following award and estimate handoff by ensuring project team structure, budget alignment, documentation, and systems are established for an effective kick-offContribute to the development and execution of the project execution plan, including critical path analysis, risk identification, resource planning, and milestone scheduling to ensure on-time milestone achievementsAdminister project changes in accordance with established change management procedures, ensuring accurate documentation and clear communication to the Client and project teamMaintain comprehensive project documentation, including the project execution plan, risk register, master schedule, health and safety plan, quality plan, and all required Rising Edge documentationMonitor project expenditures and provide regular cost reporting to the Project Manager while tracking budget performance, forecast costs, and align man-hour projections within approved budgetsCoordinate procurement activities, including materials, equipment, and subcontracted services, to ensure timely and successful project deliverySupport cash flow management by ensuring timely processing of accounts receivable and payable invoicesPrepare and maintain internal and external project reporting while facilitating clear and effective communication between the Client, project team, and key stakeholdersUnder the direction of the Project Manager(s), support project meetings including kick-offs, progress reviews, change order discussions, recovery planning, and lessons learned sessionsMonitor and report on project safety, quality and environmental compliance and acceptance requirementsAssist in identifying project staffing needs, including specialty consultants and subcontractors and support the establishment of clear roles, responsibilities, and performance expectationsSupport project close-out activities and post-project reviews to identify performance gaps and continuous improvement opportunitiesPerform additional duties as assigned by the Project Manager(s) to support the safe, efficient, and successful execution of assigned projectsAdditional Information   As a condition of employment, candidates will be required to complete pre-employment screening including professional reference checks and a criminal record check.RequirementsWhat You'll BringUndergraduate degree in Electrical Engineering, Business, Technologist or a related field requiredProject Management Professional (PMP) designation or equivalent is preferredAt least 1 - 3 years' experience managing medium to large full cycle construction projects requiredExcellent problem solving and analytical skills and a demonstrated ability to manage a diverse team in a dynamic, dead-line oriented environment are an assetProficiency in MS Project, Primavera P6, HardDollar (InEight) and ViewPoint Construction Software preferredValid class 5 driver's license with an acceptable driving record required  BenefitsWhat We Offer Competitive compensation with weekly pay periods Generous paid time off   RRSP-DPSP employer matching, increasing with years of service  Comprehensive benefits package (medical, dental, vision, and disability)  Ongoing training, development, and career advancement opportunities Employee Assistance Program (confidential counselling support) Employee Referral Bonus Program  Why Rising Edge Group?  Safety & Excellence: Safety is at the heart of everything we do. Every project is approached with diligence, strategic planning, and reliable execution practices to protect our teams and the clients we serve while delivering world-class high-voltage solutions. Our unwavering commitment to safety and quality drives excellence on every project, every day.  People-First Culture: You're more than a job title. Every person contributes to powering essential energy infrastructure across North America. Our teams are built on collaboration, respect, and accountability. We support our employees through meaningful engagement initiatives, team events, and a culture that prioritizes wellbeing, inclusion, and genuine lasting connections.Career Growth & Impact: Your role is a path to a long-term career. We empower our employees with hands-on experience, exposure to diverse projects, and opportunities to advance across disciplines, locations, and career paths. Through continuous learning, mentorship, and professional development, you'll build lasting expertise while making a tangible impact in an environment where teamwork and integrity guide our work.Reward & Recognition: We believe skill, dedication, and excellence deserves recognition. Competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits, and a strong culture of appreciation ensure your achievements are celebrated, your growth is supported, and your work truly makes a difference.  At Rising Edge Group, you're not just building projects, you're building a meaningful career where your work helps power the future, one project at a time.

title: Senior Project Manager
description: About GreystarGreystar is a leading, fully integrated global real estate platform offering expertise in property management, investment management, development, and construction services in institutional-quality rental housing. Headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina, Greystar manages and operates over $300 billion of real estate in over 260 markets globally with offices throughout North America, Europe, South America, and the Asia-Pacific region. Greystar is the largest operator of apartments in the United States, managing more than one million units/beds globally. Across its platforms, Greystar has over $79 billion of assets under management, including approximately $36 billion of development assets and over $30 billion of regulatory assets under management. Greystar was founded by Bob Faith in 1993 to become a provider of world-class service in the rental residential real estate business. To learn more, visit www.greystar.com.Job Description SummaryThe Senior Project Manager oversees multifamily construction projects from predevelopment through completion. Early responsibilities will centre on preconstruction leadership and effective management of third-party partners. As the regional pipeline expands, the role will also contribute to establishing subcontractor networks and advancing Greystar’s self-perform/general contractor capabilities. This position provides strategic and operational leadership to internal teams, consultants, general contractors, and subcontractors—ensuring all projects are executed safely, on schedule, within budget, and aligned with business objectives.Job DescriptionEssential ResponsibilitiesLead all project phases, from predevelopment through turnover and closeout. Collaborate with Development to define project scope, schedule, budget, and risk strategies. Provide early design input to ensure alignment with financial and scheduling targets. Conduct cost‑benefit and schedule analyses to support key decisions. Oversee general contractors to ensure compliance with safety, quality, budget, and schedule expectations. Perform regular site visits to assess progress, identify risks, and proactively resolve issues. Lead value engineering and constructability reviews to improve efficiency and buildability. Prepare bid packages, evaluate proposals, negotiate terms, and execute contracts. Review and validate pay applications, change orders, and cost forecasts. Ensure timely responses to submittals, RFIs, and testing documentation. Promote Greystar’s safety culture and drive zero‑incident performance. Ensure compliance with permitting requirements and oversee project closeout activities. Provide consistent reporting to stakeholders and leadership regarding project status, risks, and mitigation efforts. Support regional growth initiatives, including subcontractor development and long‑term construction infrastructure planning. Recruit, mentor, and develop team members, fostering strong performance and growth. Qualifications & RequirementsMinimum 7 years of experience managing ground‑up multifamily construction projects. Demonstrated experience in preconstruction, budgeting, and project delivery. At least 3 years of leadership experience managing subcontractors and consultants. Local estimating experience in Ontario, with the ability to contribute meaningfully to detailed underwriting for new pursuits. A strong professional network of trade partners and/or third‑party construction managers (CMs) in the region is a significant asset. Experience working across Toronto submarkets, GTA suburbs, and other Ontario markets beyond the downtown core is strongly preferred. Experience preparing institutional‑level reporting and project updates is a strong asset. Experience with both high‑rise concrete and low‑rise wood‑frame multifamily construction. Proficiency in Excel, including complex budget templates, and data analysis. Estimating experience preferred. Strong financial acumen and experience managing complex project budgets. Exceptional organizational, leadership, and decision‑making skills. Proven ability to manage risk and maintain schedule discipline across project stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including strong negotiation capabilities. Knowledge of Ontario building codes, energy standards, regulations, and permitting processes. Ability to travel to jobsites across Eastern Canada. A flexible, team‑oriented approach with openness to Greystar’s processes and ways of working. The salary range for this position is between $140,000 - $180,000 CAD.Important Notice: Greystar will never request your banking details or other sensitive personal information during the interview process. Greystar does not conduct any interviews via text or messaging, and all communication will come from official Greystar email addresses (@greystar.com). If you receive suspicious requests, please report them immediately to AskHR@greystar.com.

title: Project Coordinator – EPC
description: At Aiolos, we design and deliver some of the most advanced wind tunnel systems in the world, driving innovation for the automotive and aerospace industries. From high-performance vehicle testing to groundbreaking aerospace research, our work shapes the future of transportation and flight. Joining our team means working on complex, one-of-a-kind engineering challenges that push the limits of technology and your own potential.In the automotive sector, our climatic wind tunnels are used in the development cycle for thermodynamic testing of subsystems in production vehicles. Our aero-acoustic wind tunnels are used for aerodynamic investigations and noise studies in the automotive development process. Our design and working tools include AutoCAD, Autodesk Plant 3D, Pro Engineer Creo, MathCad and Microsoft Office Suite.Visit our website at www.aiolos.com.Position Title: Project Coordinator – EPCEmployment Type: Full-time, PermanentLocation: HybridSalary Range: $80,000-$100,000Summary:The Project Coordinator – EPC plays a critical role in supporting project managers and engineering teams to ensure projects are delivered on time, within budget and to high quality and safety standards. This role focuses on implementing best practices for planning, monitoring, reporting and controlling activities across engineering/design, procurement, construction, commissioning and project closeout phases.  Acting as a liaison between technical teams, management, and stakeholders, the Project Coordinator ensures effective communication, data management, accurate documentation and reliable forecasting. The Project Coordinator - EPC position works within the Projects group and may be assigned to a single project team or support a number of projects based on our project requirements.Aiolos designs and builds specialized high-performance test facilities, primarily wind tunnels, for automotive and aerospace industries worldwide. Based in Toronto, our team supports international clients through remote work and on-site assignments. The Project Coordinator – EPC may need to travel internationally in support of the project execution.Responsibilities/ Duties:As a direct report to the Project Manager, the Project Coordinator will participate, lead, develop, coordinate, track, support and assist in all aspects of assigned projects to ensure a safe, on-time and on-budget delivery through collaboration with the project team, company departments and external stakeholders including, but not limited to: Ø Project Planning & Scheduling:o  Identify and coordinate required contract and project deliverableso  Develop and maintain project schedules and work breakdown structures Ø Cost & Resource Management: o  Assist with budget development, resources requirements and allocationso  Develop and maintain cost breakdown structureso  Ensure progress billings (AR) are prepared, accurate and issuedo  Track procurement processes, including verification and approval of supplier (AP) invoices Ø Performance Monitoring & Reporting: o  Analyze project progress and performance, identifying trends and areas for improvement to enhance project efficiency.o  Prepare detailed status reports, variances analysis and performance metrics as per the reporting cycleso  Track actuals and prepare cash flow projectionsØ Risk & Change Management: o  Identify, collect and manage risks, issues and opportunities, including mitigation planso  Prepare and process change orders and related documentation  Ø Collaboration: o  Coordinate with business development, engineering, finance and operations support groups o  Maintain professional working relationships with all internal and external stakeholders. Assist them to resolve problems and liaise on the integration of systems.o  Participate in project meetings and provide input on status, technical and administrative matters Ø Documentation: o  Maintain accurate project records and master document registero  Prepare/Coordinate the operational manuals and project documentation and ensure it is up to date, including technical / administrative documents Ø Administer project operational activities, such as property management, correspondence, IT requirements, travel logistics, etc... Ø Perform other duties as assigned by the company Requirements/ Qualifications:·      Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Business Administration, Engineering, Project Management.·      3+ years of experience in project coordination or support roles within engineering or construction. Job-related technical knowledge necessary to perform the role·      Proven expertise in MS Excel and minimum 1 year experience with project management software and techniques, including MS Project and Microsoft Office Suite; SharePoint, Power BI and LEAN principles are assets·      Strong organizational, analytical and communication skills with time management skills to meet deadlines and manage schedules and budgets ·      A positive solution-oriented approach with desire to constantly learn, provide innovative ideas and take initiative·      Financial acumen for budgeting and cost control·      Experience in document control and revision management·      International travel and ability to accept project site assignments is considered an asset.·      Multilingual abilities (Italian is considered an asset)About UsWe are an engineering company specializing in the design and construction of test facilities for the automotive and aerospace industries. Aiolos Engineering has designed wind tunnels and test facilities for a world wide client base including: Ferrari, General Motors, Hyundai, Korean Agency for Defence Development, Indian Space Research Organization, Ford UK, Volvo, National Research Council of Canada, Scania, BMW, Daimler and NASA.

description: About Versapay 🚀Versapay turns accounts receivable (AR) into a competitive advantage.Inefficient AR processes slow cash flow and stall growth. Support other strategic priorities: Support Product leadership on strategic and operational initiatives as required.What You'll Bring To The TeamEducation: Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field; PMP or similar project management certification is a plus. This includes partnering with product, engineering, and other functions to define product roadmap and key milestones, managing beta programs, helping establish and track performance and product KPIs. Comfortable and proven experience working with clients and/ or third parties outside of internal stakeholders. Related industry experience in Payments, and/or SaaS is a strong asset. Experience in large-scale migration critical.Can-do Attitude: Optimistic style that creates positive working relationships, amplifies team culture, and fosters collaboration. Can-do attitude with out-of-the-box creativity when solving problems. Eager learner and motivated to grow. Able to navigate complex interpersonal dynamics with maturity and professionalism.Strategic mindset: Outcomes-focused mindset to determine effectiveness and ROI of programs, looking beyond the day-to-day to help reach big-picture goals and commercial levers.Customer-value thinking: Relentlessly considers the value, impacts and needs of a customer (internal or external) when considering changes, risks, decisions and paths forward. Drive and follow-through: Excellent organizational skills, work ethic, and passion to deliver on commitments. Experience in leadership roles and eager to expand this skillset. Knowledge of what needs to be done and how to work with others to help make it happen. Agility: Comfort with ambiguous and complex problems, and a flexible and open-minded approach to new challenges. Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Data fluency: Affinity to work with data and insights, synthesize data, optimize, and make recommendations to a variety of audiences. Data-driven when solving problems and ideating.Communication: Clear articulation of ideas to a variety of audiences, including at the executive level. Ability to write, speak, and present in a compelling manner.We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. Eng., PMP, GSC, and/or LEED certifications are assets.Skilled with design-build, lump sum, and construction management contractsSuccessful in building long-term relationships with clients, consultants, suppliers, and subcontractorsIn-depth knowledge of construction methods and processesStrong financial acumen and understanding of construction costsFamiliarity with local/national construction markets and labour/resource costsAble to identify improvements and recommend changesSkilled at prioritizing and managing project risksExcellent communication, negotiation, and dispute resolution skillsProficient in Microsoft Office and project management tools (e.g., CMIC, PlanGrid)Valid class G driver's licence and reliable vehicle required for site visitsYOUR ESTIMATED SALARYThis role offers an annual salary between $100,000 and $130,000, depending on experience and qualifications, with final compensation thoughtfully tailored in the offer stage.YOUR WORKING CONDITIONS AND ENVIRONMENTMain office location is 108 Vine Avenue, Toronto. Through AI and people-powered operations, this team maximizes the effectiveness, utilization, and impact of our supply, ensuring hosts and guests can seamlessly connect.The Supply Management team, within the Enterprise Operations organization, has a mission to ensure high quality supply at scale globally across homes, experiences, services, and hotels. The Engagement team, part of Supply Management, works closely with internal and cross-functional partners to onboard and support high-quality supply. Ensure campaigns are empathetic, actionable, and build host trust.Develop and document scalable campaign management processes, templates, and playbooks.Identify and implement opportunities for automation and efficiency.Set up and maintain robust tracking mechanisms to monitor campaign performance, including data collection, analytics, and reporting tools. Regularly review performance metrics, identify trends, and provide actionable insights to stakeholders.Use data and feedback to measure campaign effectiveness; identify areas for improvement and implement optimizations. Share insights and results with leadership and advise on best practices for future outreach.Oversee the selection, implementation, and ongoing maintenance of the systems, platforms, and tools required to execute outreach campaigns. Ensure these tools are properly integrated and optimized for efficiency and continuously assess them, recommending upgrades or changes to improve reliability, scalability, and effectiveness.Your Expertise10+ years’ experience in operations, program management, or campaign management, preferably in a tech or marketplace environment.Excellent project management and organizational abilities.Effective communicator with experience influencing cross-functional teams.A passion for community building and user engagement.Ability to lead and drive initiatives across a matrixed organization, solve problems, and influence cross-functional teams.Detail-oriented and strong multi-tasker with ability to manage multiple projects and initiatives simultaneously.Demonstrated experience in customer success including proactive voice outreach initiatives to engage, support, and retain clients.Demonstrated success managing multi-channel campaigns from concept through execution and analysis.Strong analytical skills; comfort with campaign dashboards and metrics.Strong communication and interpersonal skills.Comfort with ambiguity and rapid change.Experience in the tech or marketplace industry is preferred.Airtable proficiency and Salesforce expertise is preferred.Your Location:This position is Canada - Remote Eligible.
