title: Project Manager
description: About AllnorthBIG ENOUGH TO KNOW HOW, SMALL ENOUGH TO KNOW YOUAllnorth is a multi-disciplinary engineering, project delivery, and construction services company. We provide smart and practical project solutions to clients in the metals and minerals, energy and renewables, fiber and chemicals, and infrastructure sectors. With offices across Canada and into the United States, we continue to grow!Our team is our most valuable asset, and a triple bottom line (TBL) philosophy is at the heart of our business. When it comes to guiding principles, safety is part of our DNA protecting our people, our clients and our communities. It takes a talented, motivated and empowered team to delight our clients while driving profitability for all parties. Our mutual success requires that we are committed to building and nurturing Indigenous relationships, supporting our communities, and recognizing the importance of environmental stewardship.Our Ideal CandidateWe are currently looking for a Project Manager to join our team in Vancouver. As the successful candidate, you will be responsible for leading a wide variety of engineering projects, though experience with specialty structures such as bridges, is considered an asset. This role focuses on managing scope, schedule, budgets, and ensuring client expectations are consistently met and/or exceeded. This role will have a heavy focus on the commercial aspects of projects, while working with technical leads across multiple technical disciplines to coordinate work, rather than serving as a discipline-specific technical lead. Our Vancouver team works primarily on projects in the Infrastructure, Mining & Metals, and Fibre and Chemicals sectors, but also supports projects across all sectors covered by Allnorth.The successful candidate will be engaged in all levels of project delivery, beginning at proposal development and client engagement, and not ending until the completed project is turned over to the client and closed commercially. Working collaboratively with other Allnorth groups and subcontractors, you will have the opportunity to work with and lead team members to successfully deliver fast-paced projects.If your ideal role is spending years on a single mega greenfield project, this may not be the right fit. Our team thrives on variety. We work across multiple sectors and project types, from brownfield retrofits to new greenfield developments. It’s an environment where experienced professionals bring their perspective, continue to grow, and collaborate with colleagues across North America in a true One Team culture.A Day in the LifeManaging a variety of projects within the various sectors.Interfacing with clients, team members, contractors, and others to clearly define project requirements and expectations.Working with technical teams to generate proposals and project estimates.Tracking project financials, schedules, status, and change management.Ensuring completion of work within allotted schedules and budgets to high quality standards.Creating project plans that include schedules, milestones, controls, and risk management plans.Supporting safety planning for Allnorth teams.Resolving performance, scope, and schedule issues as they arise.Building business relationships with current and potential clients.Seeking continuous improvement opportunities to positively impact project and team performance.Establishing and an asset.Understanding of the timelines and expectations with construction contracting.Project Management Professional (PMP) certification or formal project management training is considered an asset.Experience leading teams of engineering and design experts within a multi-disciplined environment.A proactive approach to project management.Familiarity with Project Management Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems is an asset.Strong technical writing skills demonstrated through experience in the preparation of correspondence, reports and proposals.Effective verbal communicator, including presentation, facilitation and listening skills.Highly organized with strong attention to detail and quality.Results-oriented with the capacity to successfully manage multiple competing priorities and timelines.Superior analytical, problem-solving, audit and investigation skills.A proven ability to collaborate with team members and clients in a multi-disciplined environment.Excellent organizational skills and a strong attention to detail.Registered or eligible to register as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng) in the province of British Columbia is not required but considered an asset.What’s in it for you?We believe that a happy and fulfilled team leads to exceptional results, which is why we offer a range of perks designed to support your well-being, career growth and community engagement.Comprehensive benefits package includes extended health and dental coverage and RRSP matchingEmployee Assistance Program for team members and their familiesA culture of respect and inclusivity fosters long-term relationships and a team atmosphereProfessional development and growth opportunitiesTeam member referral program recruiting incentivesMonthly “Lunch & Learn” sessionsOpportunities to bond with other team members, have fun and make a difference through involvement in social and safety committees, as well as fundraising and community support initiatives (e.g. Movember, Pi Day, Food Banks, Women’s Shelters, Ronald McDonald House, Earth Day clean ups, etc.).WE TAKE CARE OF YOU SO YOU CAN TAKE CARE OF OUR CLIENTSIn joining us you’ll face rewarding, career-developing challenges that enable you to contribute to our clients’ successes, and despite how much we grow, you’ll never be just a number, but rather a vital member of the Allnorth Team.Engage with us at Allnorth and be empowered to enrich people’s lives, including your own.

title: Project Coordinator, Ontario Line North (Project Subways)
description: Job DescriptionContract term up to 12 months Start rate (non-negotiable): $49.68Metrolinx is connecting communities across the Greater Golden Horseshoe. Metrolinx operates GO Transit and UP Express, as well as the PRESTO fare payment system. We are also building new and improved rapid transit, including GO Expansion, Light Rail Transit routes, and major expansions to Toronto’s subway system, to get people where they need to go, better, faster and easier. Metrolinx is an agency of the Government of Ontario.At Metrolinx, equity, diversity and inclusion are essential to living our values of serving with passion, thinking forward and playing as a team.CPG’s workforce is made up of driven and dedicated professionals who care deeply about working together to deliver infrastructure and make a positive impact in our communities. Join us as we build a knowledgeable and collaborative team that courageously changes how transit projects will be delivered.Our Capital Projects Group, Environmental Programs and Assessment (EPA) division is looking for Environmental Project Coordinators to join the team to coordinate environmental assessments for planned infrastructure projects and coordinate the environmental components of approved projects in the implementation (design and construction) phase. This involves coordinating the relationship between the various stakeholders, consultants, and contractors involved in our transportation infrastructure projects that require environmental approvals, strategic advice and support, mitigation, and reporting. Successful candidates may be assigned to multiple projects.What will I be doing?Plan, manage, and oversee the project work of consultants and contractors, to ensure environmental compliance with Environmental Assessment Requirements as prescribed in the Ontario Regulation 341/20: Ontario Line Project, Transit and Rail Project Assessment Process (TRPAP), and Impact Assessment Act (Federal) and report internal user group requirements through all project stages, such as the preparation of environmental assessment documents, mitigation reports, project studies, design reports, sustainable development reports, accessibility reports, contract specifications and construction administration including the review of associated technical studies on social and biophysical environments (e.g., noise, vibration, air quality, built heritage, natural heritage, archaeology, design, and mitigation).Provide information, assistance, and advice (by explaining procedures and information needs) on legislative requirements as they pertain to projects.Monitoring and reporting on contractor / consultant adherence to contract terms of reference, and compliance with the Ministry of the Environment, Conservation & Parks (MECP) and Conservation Authority guidelines and assisting in the development and application of corrective measures.Ensuring O. Reg. 341/20, TRPAP and GO Transit Class EA conditions are achieved and reporting requirements are met.Conduct research and prepare documents for approval and recommend approval terms and conditions to the project team.Review and interpretation of existing and new codes, guidelines, and legislation impacting environmental compliance; convey this information in writing to relevant internal stakeholders.Identify policy, procedure, and information gaps, and develop recommendations for improvements and/or corrective action.Develop good internal and external working relationships, to collaborate and exchange information and maintain a current knowledge base of policies and legislation.Assist Metrolinx management and the communication group by providing information, interpretation, and clarification on regulatory requirements and approvals.Manage the environmental mitigation and reporting aspects of projects through all stages of design and construction in terms of function, quality schedules and approvals in co-ordination with external authorities and internal groups.Administer consultant agreements and construction contracts as they pertain to environmental mitigation and reporting of Metrolinx infrastructure and expansion projects.Conduct regular site inspections at various project locations to ensure environmental compliance.What Skills and Qualifications Do I Need?A university/college diploma in Environmental/Civil Engineering or a related discipline (Planning/Environmental Management/Environmental Science), or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience providing a thorough knowledge of the theories and practices of environmental monitoring and/or environmental planning/management.Minimum 5 years’ experience related to Environmental Assessments and compliance within the planning, permitting, design and construction of transit facilities, interpreting and applying contract plans and specifications and co-ordinating contracted engineering projects. Knowledge of Ontario and Canadian regulatory requirements including fields relating to noise and vibration, air quality, contamination, built heritage, natural heritage (e.g., species at risk).Experience working with regulatory bodies (e.g., federal agencies, provincial ministries, Conservation Authorities, municipalities) and other stakeholder groups.Proficiency in the use of project management software and computer applications is essential. Detailed knowledge of project management including cost control, scheduling, codes, standards, specifications, corporate policies and procedures and applicable environmental legislation. Thorough understanding of the O. Reg. 341/20, Transit and Rail Project Assessment Process, Ontario and Canadian Environmental Assessment processes, Environmental Protection Act, Endangered Species Act, Migratory Birds Convention Act, Planning Act and MTO Class Environmental Assessment. Excellent written and oral communication skills, ability to work independently, strong interpersonal and negotiating skills and analytical ability are a must.A valid Ontario Class G driver’s license is required to travel to various site locations, meetings, operational facilities, etc. Must meet and maintain the corporate standard for a good driving record.Canadian Certified Inspector of Sediment and Erosion Control (CAN-CISEC) certification and/or demonstrated proficiency in inspecting construction sites for compliance with Erosion and Sediment Control (ESC) plans are considered an asset. AccommodationWe value the unique skills and experiences each person brings to Metrolinx and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment. We are committed to the requirements of the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act so if you require accommodation during the hiring process, please let our Recruitment team know by contacting us at: 416-202-5601 or email hr.recruitment@metrolinx.com.Application ProcessAll applicants must be legally entitled to work in Canada. Metrolinx will be using email to communicate with you for all job competitions. It is your responsibility to include an updated email address that is checked daily and accepts emails from unknown users. As we send time-sensitive correspondence, we recommend that you check your email regularly. If no response is received, we will assume you are no longer interested in pursuing the opportunity. Please be advised that a Criminal Record Check may be required of the successful candidate.Should it be determined that any background information provided is misleading, inaccurate or incorrect, Metrolinx reserves the right to discontinue with the consideration of your application.We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.WE ARE AN EQUITABLE AND INCLUSIVE EMPLOYER.

description: Infrastructure Ontario (IO), an Ontario crown corporation, provides a wide range of services to support the Ontario government’s initiative to modernize and maximize the value of public infrastructure and realty. IO has been recognized domestically and internationally for the way it does business and the success it has had with its major projects, real estate services, infrastructure lending, commercial projects, and development mandate. title: MTO Project Manager
description: Join Egis, where your ambition meets unparalleled growth opportunities. As we continue to expand within Canada, we're looking for talented professionals like you to be a part of our innovative team. Imagine working alongside over 1,000 top-tier engineers, architects, and planners, all dedicated to shaping the future of infrastructure and development. At Egis, your skills and aspirations will find the perfect environment to thrive. Elevate your career with us and be a driving force in our exciting journey. Our values showcase everything we believe in: A Responsible Company - We care about people, and we care about our impact; acting responsibly wherever we work and whatever we do.   A People-First Company - This is an environment of mutual respect where every team member matters, no matter who they are.  A Creative Company - Creativity is in our DNA. This is the fuel that helps us tackle the major challenges of our time.   Job DescriptionThe Role Our highway engineering team delivers a diverse range of MTO design projects, encompassing highway expansion, interchange design, roadway rehabilitation, and bridge rehabilitation/replacements. With a keen focus on serving the Ministry of Transportation (MTO), our team navigates the complexities of each project with precision and expertise. Leveraging our extensive experience and proficiency, we consistently meet the evolving demands, ensuring the safe and efficient flow of traffic across Ontario's road networks. One of the key factors setting our department apart is the relationship we've cultivated with the MTO team over the years. This close collaboration enables us to navigate regulatory frameworks effectively and streamline project delivery processes, giving us a competitive edge in the industry. Furthermore, our commitment to fostering a culture of collaboration shines through in our approach, as we excel in working closely with all project stakeholders and multi-disciplinary teams. This collaborative approach enhances project outcomes and fosters innovation and efficiency throughout the project lifecycle. In showcasing our capabilities, we take pride in our proven track record of successful project execution. From freeway widening and interchange improvements in the GTA and surrounding area, to roadway rehabilitation initiatives spanning Northern Ontario and intersection and roundabout designs in Southwestern Ontario, our portfolio exemplifies our commitment to excellence and our ability to deliver impactful solutions that enhance Ontario's transportation infrastructure. Join our team and contribute to shaping the future of highway engineering in Ontario with your expertise and passion for project management. Key Responsibilities: Acting as a project manager on multidiscipline detail design highway and bridge projects for MTO. Providing mentorship, support, and oversight to other engineers and technical staff working on your projects. Supporting and leading a project team to complete designs ranging from freeways, urban and rural multi-lane and two-lane facilities, interchanges, and intersections. Ensuring that corporate and client QA/QC practices are completed on all projects. Coordinating with clients, approval agencies, utility companies, contractors, etc. Developing and maintaining relationships with industry professionals, consultants and other business leaders. Preparing proposals, project work plans, schedules, and budgets. Monitoring overall work progress and technical performance on projects to ensure timelines and targets are being met. QualificationsHas developed an excellent reputation working with MTO. A university degree in Civil Engineering. Licensed as a Professional Engineer (P.Eng.). 15-20 years of progressive related experience working on MTO design projects in highway design and project management. Experienced with project management and has worked in or has an understanding of the consulting engineering environment. Thorough knowledge of Highway/Transportation Engineering, including policies, regulations, guidelines and operational procedures. Demonstrated experience in design work with proven project management capabilities. Ability to work effectively as part of a team with clients and other relevant stakeholders. Excellent oral and written communication skills. Strong attention to detail, problem-solving skills and ability to think creatively. Excellent interpersonal skills and an ability to work effectively independently or as part of a team. Possess a valid driver’s license and have access to a vehicle with valid registration and insurance. Ability to be approved in the MTO RAQS system for various specialties would be considered an asset. Additional InformationWe welcome and encourage applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process.This role is eligible for a referral bonus in line with the Referral Program.  If you have been referred, please provide the name of the employee who has referred you at the time of your application.At Egis, we embrace innovation to tackle challenges and utilize AI in our recruitment process under stringent ethics and compliance policies and in alignment with our values of being a Responsible Company, a People First Company and a Creative Company. Committed to diversity, growth and collaboration, we may leverage AI to support the recruitment process.Egis Canada is an equal opportunities employer offering a competitive salary and benefits to those with the skills suited to the needs of our clients. The salary range advertised may exceed the range based on several factors, including relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, geographic location, performance, and business or organizational needs.

Business RequirementsExceptional time management and organizational skills are requiredDesire to work well in a fast-paced, deadline-driven, and customer-focused environment Cultural Requirements Ability to lead a construction management team is requiredExcellent communication and interpersonal skills with individuals of various disciplinesA team player with the desire to work collaboratively with others to improve existing processes As a member of the OSCO Construction Group, FCC offers a comprehensive compensation package including health and dental coverage, life insurance, RRSP and tax-free savings account options. Additionally, we offer educational scholarships to dependents of employees, health & wellness programming and team celebratory events. Since that time, the OSCO Construction Group has grown to encompass four main operating sectors: Steel, Concrete, Construction and Corporate. Within these sectors lie an ever-expanding number of construction-related companies and divisions, serving a growing market area and employing over twelve hundred employees.

Projects in this division typically include ground-up builds, major renovations, and complex ICI construction.Who We Are:Prodigy is a North American Construction Management firm based in Toronto, Canada, with offices in Vancouver, Montreal, and Chicago. We specialize in Institutional, Retail, Hospitality, and Corporate construction, with a focus on the North American market.At Prodigy, our people serve as the driving force behind our path towards greatness. This collective dedication, encompassing of our executive team, senior management, and our highly talented field-level professionals, has propelled Prodigy to rapid success and a solid industry reputation. In doing so, we've built a diverse and inclusive team, where each members unique experiences and perspectives enrich our collective journey. Our commitment is to ensure that every team member feels empowered to embrace their authentic selves, and to provide every team member with the opportunity to reach their full potential and make a meaningful contribution to the communities we serve.Please click HERE to learn more about our organization.As a Project Manager, you’ll be responsible for:Leadership & Project OversightProvide full leadership and accountability for assigned projects from start-up to closeout.Uphold and champion Prodigy’s values, mission, brand standards, and commitment to Health & Safety at all times.Effectively communicate with clients, consultants, internal teams, and trade partners to maintain alignment and drive project success.Project Planning & ExecutionLead project planning, scheduling, procurement, budgeting, and financial tracking.Ensure all contractual requirements are met, including quality, safety, schedule, and cost objectives.Develop and implement risk management and mitigation strategies.Drive schedule recovery efforts and corrective action plans when required.Ensure project documentation is accurate, current, and compliant with company standards.People & Relationship ManagementBuild and maintain strong relationships with clients, consultants, trades, and internal stakeholders.Mentor and support project staff across cross-functional teams.Support business development by identifying opportunities and participating in proposal efforts where required.Field & Site SupportCollaborate closely with Project Superintendents to ensure effective site execution.Support site teams with timely decisions, clarifications, and issue resolution.Foster positive working relationships with trades, suppliers, and field personnel.About You:The successful candidate will have the following:Minimum of 7+ years of experience in Project Management roles with a General Contractor in the ICI construction sector.Practical experience with Construction Management and Stipulated Sum contract models; Design-Build, IPD, and hybrid models considered an asset.Strong stakeholder relationship management skills.Awareness of current industry trends, best practices, and construction methodologies.Ability to independently manage several smaller projects or one larger project.Proficiency in MS Office Suite, MS Project, accounting/cost-tracking systems (Jonas or similar), Procore, Bluebeam, and related industry software.Proven ability to meet project objectives related to schedule, budget, and quality.High degree of resourcefulness, flexibility, and adaptability.Strong work ethic with the ability to persevere through challenges.Exceptional organizational and time management skills.Highly self-motivated with the ability to prioritize and execute tasks in a fast-paced environment.Strong verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills at all levels.Ability to interpret and apply contract drawings and specifications.Demonstrated experience in resolving conflicts effectivelyPerks & Benefits:Competitive pay and vacation packageAnnual performance based bonusEmployer paid Health & Dental benefitsA fun, people-first culture with regular social events and celebrationsApproximate Annual Salary Range for this role: $100,000 - $150,000Final compensation is determined based on experience, certifications, education/training, work location, and the size and the complexity of the projects assigned.This posting is for an existing vacancy within our organization.Artificial Intelligence (AI) Use:We may use technology to support the initial application review; however, final decisions are always made by our hiring team after reviewing applications and completing interviews.To Apply:Ready to take the next step in your career? Submit your resume today! We know that fit for a role goes both ways, and you may have other questions – so feel free to contact us in advance of applying.Prodigy Group is strongly committed to an inclusive selection process, being barrier-free, and fostering a workforce that reflects the diversity of the community. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals, including applicants from all cultures, racialized communities, abilities, diverse sexual and gender identities, and others who may contribute to the further diversification of ideas. Prodigy Group provides accommodation, accessible formats, and communication support for the recruitment process upon request. Please contact us at hr@prodigy-group.com if you require accommodation at any stage in the recruitment process.Connect with us on LinkedIn and Instagram to stay updated on our exciting projects and company culture.

title: Project Manager, Operations
description: About Nicola Wealth:We have a lot to be proud of at Nicola Wealth. Our sophisticated financial planning, our diversified private investment funds and their exceptional performance, our 98% client retention rate and our award-winning culture, combine to position us as a national leader in wealth management. Recognized as a Best Managed Company and one of Canada’s Top Employers, we are dedicated to upholding our firm’s values through this phase of significant growth.By bringing together smart, dedicated and supportive colleagues from diverse backgrounds, we deliver extraordinary client service in a values-driven work environment. The collaboration, creativity and entrepreneurial spirit we were founded on provides the opportunity for you to make a real impact and build a rewarding career. We are passionate about our business, our culture and investing deeply in your growth and development to build for the future. If you have this same drive, then let’s talk!Purpose of the Role::Reporting to the Director, Operations, the Project Manager, Operations focuses on business consultation and project management to support operational initiatives to drive the continuous improvement and scalability of our business. This role requires broad stakeholder engagement and project management experience and the ability to lead projects from concept to completion.Core Responsibilities:Lead and deliver projects of varying size and complexity using appropriate methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid), ensuring alignment with strategic goals and timely execution. Demonstrate flexibility and adaptability in managing both large-scale initiatives and smaller, fast-moving projects, adjusting approach and tools as needed. Manage and report on project activities, including risks, issues, and decisions. Drive conversations with Executive Sponsors to clarify priorities, obtain time from subject matter experts (SMEs), and validate recommendations. Work with Project Sponsors to document and confirm project objectives, benefits, and inter-dependencies. Facilitate effective communication across teams, ensuring transparency, alignment, and timely resolution of issues or blockers. Conduct hands-on process and business analysis, including workflow mapping, data analysis, and root cause investigation to support informed decision-making and continuous improvement. Collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders to gather requirements, define project goals, and translate business needs into actionable plans. Champion operational excellence, identifying opportunities for efficiency and scalability within existing workflows. Monitor and report on project performance, using metrics and insights to drive accountability and optimize outcomes, and quantify the benefits of implemented changes. Support change management by obtaining agreement from leadership and business owners to implement new processes, tools or systems, and tracking the impact of those changes. Required Skills & Qualifications:Minimum of 3 – 5 years of project management experience or equivalent, preferably in the financial services industry. Bachelor’s Degree in a related discipline. Project Management education and/or certifications are an asset. Solid understanding of the wealth management industry, financial markets, financial planning, asset management, and/or insurance. Experience with project management/delivery software. Highly proficient with word processing, spreadsheet, and presentation software. Exposure to change management and/or process optimization is an assetCompensation & Benefits Package:This position comes with a base salary range of $80,000 - $100,000 plus a competitive performance bonus, RRSP contribution and participation in our profit-sharing program. Base salary will be commensurate with experience, skills, and qualifications.At Nicola Wealth, our people grow our business, delight our clients, and live our values every day. Our comprehensive compensation and benefits package reflects our appreciation for this commitment. Whether it’s participating in our profit sharing and benefit programs on day one, or the opportunity to participate in our share ownership program, our people are able to go beyond a conventional salary and bonus structure. To learn more about our complete package, visit: www.nicolawealth.com/total-rewards.This role is a current vacancy within Nicola Wealth.Please include both a cover letter and resume in support of your application. We are an inclusive, equal opportunity employer. For more information about this and other roles: www.nicolawealth.com/careers.It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. Should you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process please contact us at accessibility@nicolawealth.com and we will work with you to meet your needs. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Nicola Wealth policies.Thank you for your interest in a career with Nicola Wealth!

Strong knowledge of road construction, including paving, sub-grade, gravel base course, concrete construction, and related subcontractor operations.Travel Requirements: Frequent travel between job sites within the City of Edmonton and surrounding areasAdditional RequirementsMonitor project costs and financial reports, providing valuable feedback to the operations and estimating teams.Act as the direct liaison with clients, consultants, and owners to ensure customer satisfaction and successful project delivery.Work with the accounting team to meet cash collection and financial targets.Assist in preparing monthly progress invoices, reviewing accruals, and approving supplier and subcontractor invoices.For smaller projects, ensure project plans, coding, and locates are distributed to supervisors and crews in a timely manner.Successful candidates must adhere to all safety protocols and proper use of Amrize approved Personal Protection Equipment ("PPE"), including but not limited to respirators. title: Project Manager
description: Who We Are Rising Edge Group is an electrical contracting company founded in 2002, specialization in high-voltage electrical solutions that power critical infrastructure across North America. With operations in both Canada and the United States, we partner with clients across the electrical utilities, renewable energy, and industrial sectors to design, build, and maintain the critical infrastructure systems communities and industries rely on.  Driven by a clear vision to strengthen the stability, reliability, and advancement of energy infrastructure, we take pride in delivering work that matters. Guided by our STRIDE values - Safety, Teamwork, Responsibility, Integrity, Deliver, and Excellence - we foster a people focused on a culture of growth, mentorship and collaboration where safety always come first. What You'll Do Reporting to the Program Manager, the Project Manager is responsible for the overall management, coordination and delivery of assigned projects. The Project Manager ensures that the project is delivered safely, on time, within budget and meeting the required scope and quality specifications. This role is responsible to continuously communicate with and manage expectations of clients and other project stakeholders. As a project leader during the entire lifecycle of a project, the Project Manager is accountable to provide routine project progress and ad hoc reports to senior management. Key ResponsibilitiesThe responsibilities include, but may not be limited to: Initiate project setup following award and estimate handoff, ensuring appropriate team structure, budget alignment, documentation, and systems are in place to support an effective project kick-offDevelop, implement, and maintain the project execution plan, including milestone schedules, critical path analysis, allocate and manage resources, and apply risk mitigation strategies to ensure timely achievement of project deliverablesAdminister project changes in accordance with established change management procedures. Ensure all scope, schedule, and cost impacts are documented, approved, and clearly communicated to the Client and project teamMaintain comprehensive project documentation, including the project execution plan, risk register, schedule, health and safety plan, quality plan, and all Rising Edge mandated documentationMonitor and control project financial performance, ensuring adherence to approved budgets and alignment of man-hour projections with actual performanceOversee and coordinate procurement of materials, equipment, and subcontracted services to ensure timely and cost-effective delivery of project requirementsEnsure timely processing and reconciliation of accounts receivable and accounts payable to support project cash flow managementProvide consistent and accurate project reporting and communication across all parties such as Clients and internal stakeholders to lead project meetings, including kick-offs, progress updates, change order reviews, recovery planning sessions, and lessons learned discussionsMaintain continuous oversight of project safety and environmental compliance requirements, ensuring alignment with regulatory and organizational standardsIdentify and secure appropriate project staffing resources, including specialty consultants and subcontractors. Establish clear performance expectations and accountability to support successful project deliveryLead project closeout activities, including documentation completion, financial reconciliation, and post-project evaluations. Conduct post-mortem reviews to identify performance gaps and implement continuous improvement recommendationsRequirementsWhat You'll BringUndergraduate degree in Electrical Engineering, Business, Technologist or a related field requiredProject Management Professional (PMP) designation or equivalent required??At least 3 - 5 years' experience managing medium to large full cycle construction projects requiredExcellent problem solving and analytical skills and a demonstrated ability to manage a diverse team in a dynamic, dead-line oriented environment are an assetProficiency in MS Project, Primavera P6, HardDollar(InEight) and ViewPoint Construction Software preferredValid class 5 driver's license with an acceptable driving record requiredAdditional Information   As a condition of employment, candidates will be required to complete pre-employment screening including professional reference checks and a criminal record check.BenefitsWhat We Offer Competitive compensation with weekly pay periods Generous paid time off   RRSP-DPSP employer matching, increasing with years of service  Comprehensive benefits package (medical, dental, vision, and disability)  Ongoing training, development, and career advancement opportunities Employee Assistance Program (confidential counselling support) Employee Referral Bonus Program    Why Rising Edge Group?  Safety & Excellence: Safety is at the heart of everything we do. Every project is approached with diligence, strategic planning, and reliable execution practices to protect our teams and the clients we serve while delivering world-class high-voltage solutions. Our unwavering commitment to safety and quality drives excellence on every project, every day.  People-First Culture: You're more than a job title. Every person contributes to powering essential energy infrastructure across North America. Our teams are built on collaboration, respect, and accountability. We support our employees through meaningful engagement initiatives, team events, and a culture that prioritizes wellbeing, inclusion, and genuine lasting connections.Career Growth & Impact: Your role is a path to a long-term career. We empower our employees with hands-on experience, exposure to diverse projects, and opportunities to advance across disciplines, locations, and career paths. Through continuous learning, mentorship, and professional development, you'll build lasting expertise while making a tangible impact in an environment where teamwork and integrity guide our work.Reward & Recognition: We believe skill, dedication, and excellence deserves recognition. Competitive compensation, comprehensive benefits, and a strong culture of appreciation ensure your achievements are celebrated, your growth is supported, and your work truly makes a difference.  At Rising Edge Group, you're not just building projects, you're building a meaningful career where your work helps power the future, one project at a time.

title: Project Manager
description: CentreCourt is seeking a Project Manager to join the project team at Pickering City Centre, a mixed-use development in Pickering’s downtown core. Pickering City Centre is a 55-acre, mixed-use, master-planned community that will ultimately deliver 6,000+ units, a revitalized retail destination anchored by The Shops at Pickering City Centre, along with new public spaces and enhanced pedestrian connectivity. This landmark project marks a defining chapter in CentreCourt’s growing portfolio and offers a rare opportunity to gain hands-on experience on a complex, large-scale urban development. This is an exciting time to join a high-performing team known for execution, excellence, and results.The Project Manager will work closely with internal teams, consultants, contractors, municipal authorities, and senior leadership to successfully deliver a complex, large-scale high-rise residential project within the broader Pickering City Centre master plan. This role is ideally suited to a construction professional who thrives in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment and brings strong technical expertise, leadership capability, and a proactive, solutions-oriented mindset.Key Responsibilities:Project Planning & Execution  Provide leadership and guidance to project staff while assisting in managing day-to-day site and project operations.Develop, implement, and manage comprehensive construction project plans, schedules, and budgets to ensure projects are completed on time and within budget.Assist in developing detailed project schedules with key milestones and continuously monitor trade progress against baseline schedules.Ensure project schedules are accurate, maintained, and submitted on time.Manage all aspects of pre-construction, including preparing tenders and writing detailed scopes of work specific to plans and specifications.Analyze bids, prepare bid recaps, and participate in the awarding of contracts.Assist in the development of construction management plans, including organizing utility disconnects and securing site offices.Identify and manage long-lead items to ensure timely procurement and delivery. Consultant & Stakeholder Management  Manage architects, engineers, and consultants to ensure completeness, coordination, and constructability of design documents.Act as a liaison between construction sites and various CentreCourt office teams, ensuring alignment and timely information flow.Coordinate all City inspections, approvals, and occupancy requirements Financial Management & Reporting  Prepare monthly project progress and status reports, including updated budgets, accurate cost-to-complete forecasts, and schedule comparisons to baseline.Review and co-approve progress draws and general invoices.Investigate, analyze, and document all extras and credits to contract; maintain extra/credit logs and prepare Change Orders for execution and approval.Identify and report material cost, schedule, or scope issues in a timely manner.Construction Oversight & Quality Control  Conduct periodic on-site inspections to ensure construction activities meet CentreCourt standards, approved drawings, and schedules.Provide recommendations for corrective actions or process improvements when required.Ensure all activities are completed on schedule, including obtaining IFC drawings, building permits, and confirming availability of all required services (hydro, gas, water, telecommunications).Occupancy, Deficiencies & Close-Out  Ensure suite construction inspection and deficiency rectification programs are implemented and completed prior to PDI.Assist with managing the condominium common element process and Performance Audits, ensuring deficiencies are corrected within required timelines.Coordinate and manage all City occupancy milestones through to final completion.Health & Safety  Assist the Site Superintendent in implementing, managing, and enforcing health and safety programs in accordance with legislative and CentreCourt requirements. Candidate Qualifications:University or college degree in Engineering, Architecture, Construction Management, or a related field.Approximately 6+ years of experiencein high-rise residential construction and development.Proven experience completing 2-3 high-rise residential projects from pre-construction through occupancy.Strong working knowledge of construction methodologies, scheduling, budgeting, and contract administration.Proficiency in Microsoft Office, Microsoft Project, Excel, and Procore.Demonstrated ability to analyze site plans, building drawings, suite layouts, and technical documentation.Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail.Ability to perform effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities simultaneously.Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to lead diverse teams and interface with senior stakeholders.About CentreCourtCentreCourt is a fully integrated real estate development firm active across all stages of the development lifecycle – land acquisition, zoning, design, marketing, sales/leasing, construction, and customer care. We specialize in high-rise condominiums and purpose-built rental communities located near major transit, amenities, and employment hubs in the Greater Toronto Area.Recognized as one of Canada’s fastest-growing companies, CentreCourt has delivered over 10,000 homes and $5 billion in development value since 2010. In 2022, we launched CentreCourt Platform II, a $400 million fully committed long-term equity fund that provides the financial strength and flexibility to support our continued growth and to act decisively in a dynamic market environment.We operate as a flat, entrepreneurial, and meritocratic organization that rewards high performance with exceptional growth opportunities. Our culture values humility, continuous improvement, and unwavering integrity.You can learn more at www.centrecourt.com.CentreCourt is an equal opportunity employer, committed to providing an inclusive and accessible workplace. In accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, accommodations are available upon request at any stage of the recruitment and selection process.  We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

title: Project Manager - Construction
description: Reporting to the Senior Project Manager, the Project Manager leads store development projects across the JOEY Restaurant Group. This role partners closely with internal teams, external contractors, and Operations to drive project delivery from planning through construction and store reopening. You are responsible for scheduling, budgeting, submitting permits, administration, and quality control, ensuring projects are executed on time, on budget, and to JOEY Restaurant Group's high standards. Preference will be given to candidates based in our Vancouver, BC office, with the potential for remote work from Calgary or Edmonton with occasional travel to active project sites.The ideal candidate is a proactive leader with strong organization, clear communication, and a commitment to excellence. You guide project teams, manage stakeholders, and document learnings to continuously improve the new store development process and elevate the JOEY guest and team experience.Annual Compensation Range$85,000 - $115,000 / yearThe salary range is dependent on skill and work experience.ResponsibilitiesManage multiple store development projects from project commencement through construction and store reopening. Lead the project team (internal and external partners) by defining and communicating the project tasks, as well as processing and tracking milestones within the project cycle. Coordinate with in-house Real Estate stakeholders on the leasing agreement, landlord scope of work and project schedule to ensure the delivery of the new store within committed lease obligations. Develop the project milestone schedule and lead the project team to meet scheduled dates. Establish, and evaluate project estimates and budgets using inputs from all project partners, and project historical data. Act as ‘Owner Representative' to supervise the project partners such as the General Contractor, Foodservice partner and Consultants during planning and construction Work together with the Project Accountant to track all project costs, timely payment of contracts and invoices, and report weekly on project costs. Communicate project roadblocks to Director of Construction throughout the project lifecycle and leverage partners to resolve these roadblocks. Facilitate a deficiency-free project handover to Facilities and Operations, ensuring they have the support needed for a successful store reopening. Implement best practices in coordination with all stakeholders from lessons learned on every project and feedback given by all stakeholders. Manage project close-out of all documentation, site deficiencies, and project financials with the assistance of the Project Accountant, including the submission of all required documentation to the Landlord to ensure timely release of the Tenant Improvement Allowance. Partner with Facilities, Operations and all project partners for any warranty issues and report findings for future best practices accordingly. As required, manage large scale Capital Investment projects and renovations RequirementsYou bring 3+ years of project management experience, ideally within construction environmentsExperience in the commercial hospitality or retail industry preferred. Advance knowledge and experience working with MS365 required. Experience with MS Project preferred. Post-Secondary Education, in Construction, Design or Engineering related programs (preferred but not required). PMP designation an assetExceptional communication skills both verbal and written. A natural problem solver, thinking outside the box to execute reasonable solutions while maintaining schedule and budget. Strong attention to detail and process flow. Highly capable in negotiation and mediation of conflict resolution. Highly organized and self-motivated in planning and performance control. High performer under stress and high-pressure situations. Retains focus and traction on the entire cycle of a Project from project commencement through construction and store opening. Takes ownership and initiative to execute successful project. BenefitsExtended Health, Dental, Life Insurance, Long Term Disability, AD&D, Critical Illness coverage Ongoing Leadership Development Courses Enjoy 50% off Food & Beverages at any JOEY Restaurant, LOCAL Public Eatery, SALTLIK or Earls Restaurant location (some restrictions apply) Pursue personal and professional development, through a Coaching Partnership. Being paired with a coach will give you ongoing support and feedback so that you are able to pursue your life goals. Industry partner incentives Referral bonus programs This is about you. Your attitude, passion, and potential. When you join our corporate office team, you can expect to be a part of something big. Whether in Finance, Marketing, Payroll, IT or Design, you will find yourself working alongside the industry's best talent. As individuals, we are ambitious and driven, curious and engaged. Together, we are a high-performing team committed to each other's development and growth, and the success of over 50 Restaurant locations throughout North America.What does this mean for you? It means if you're looking for a place to meet your potential, we'd like to meet you. Your journey starts here.JOEY Restaurant Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity EmployerJOEY Restaurant Group provides reasonable accommodations to qualified applicants and employees, including due to disabilities, medical conditions, and religious beliefs and practices.

title: IT Project Manager
description: Who are we?For over 40 years, Bath Fitter has been perfecting its process to meet the needs and provide the best bathtubs for everyone. Our showers and bathtubs are made of shiny acrylic that is easy to clean and can be installed in just one day. Convenient! Every day, the people at Bath Fitter strive to do better; to improve products and customers' lives. Our vision is to be an excellent employer and business partner.At Bath Fitter, we offer an engaged culture, a collaborative environment focused on mutual support, autonomy, and innovation. We are a growing organization that prioritizes employees and their quality of life. That's why we're proud to be certified as " Great Place to Work" & "Most Admired Corporate Cultures in Canada."In summary, what does the role entail?As a Project Manager, you will be at the heart of delivering strategic, large-scale projects supported by the IT team. With your leadership skills and mastery of Agile and Waterfall methodologies, you will ensure clear project scope, optimal planning, and rigorous deadline tracking. You will analyze business needs, prioritize the backlog, and orchestrate sprint planning. You will also play a key role in communication and project governance, ensuring follow-up with stakeholders and leading all project meetings with IT teams and business partners.Why become a part of the Bath Fitter Team?Flexible schedule;Group insurance;Retirement plan with employer contribution;Paid vacation, personal days, and additional holidays during the festive season;Employee & corporate discounts;Develop expertise in Canadian and American markets;Hold a key position within the team;Gym at the St-Eustache headquarters;Bistro that will make you want to come to the office (varied, delicious meals subsidized by BathFitter);Employee assistance program;Social club;Many other benefits to discover by joining Bath Fitter!What is the ideal profile for this position?5 to 7 years of experience in project management, with practical knowledge of Agile and Waterfall methodologies. Experience with ERP and/or CRM applications, as well as a solid understanding of retail management, customer experience, and/or manufacturing systems within an IT ecosystem. Professional accreditation in project management (PMP, PRINCE2, etc.) is a plus. Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field is a plus. Excellent leadership and collaboration skills with stakeholders at all levels, with strategic maturity to manage executive expectations. Ability to prioritize, meet deadlines, document projects effectively, manage multiple initiatives simultaneously, and work in a dynamic environment with rigor and organization. Excellent communication skills, proficiency in French is an asset. As a Project Manager, IT, what will your responsibilities be? Support the program manager in the ongoing adoption of the project management framework and best practices across various business sectors. Identify and implement project management best practices within the established framework for each project. Manage multiple projects by adapting both Waterfall and Agile methodologies to the project framework. Develop and manage the project plan, monitoring scope, budget, and effort to ensure timely delivery and quality of deliverables. Lead and participate in business analysis activities, including requirements gathering workshops and backlog management. Manage the project RAID log and establish mitigation plans for identified risks. Establish and develop excellent business relationships with internal and external stakeholders. Facilitate steering committee meetings and daily scrum ceremonies to ensure projects are on track and oversee change management activities to ensure positive user adoption. We believe life is too short to not be passionate about what we do every day. description: WHAT ON EARTH IS DECIEM?We are a collective of disrupters and doers, of creatives and scientists, with a mission to reimagine the world of beauty.Since inception, our vertically integrated structure, including our own laboratories, manufacturing, retail stores, and brand infrastructure, have enabled us to rapidly identify opportunities and deliver quality. Because normal gets you nowhere.We are DECIEM, The Abnormal Beauty Company.We are growing rapidly, and we’re looking for someone who shares this vision and wants to grow with us!WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FORWe’re looking for a strategic, detail-oriented, hands-on Senior Project Management leader who will own and drive DECIEM’s product development roadmap from R&D through to launch.You’ll bring structure, clarity, and momentum across complex, cross-functional projects, ensuring delivery on time, on budget, and to the highest standards.This role is all about turning strategy into execution, proactively managing risk, and elevating how we deliver products at scale.If you thrive in fast-paced environments and love bringing teams together to make great ideas real, we’d love to meet you 💛This is a hybrid role based out of our Toronto office.PAY RANGEThe anticipated base salary for this position is $100k to $110KExact salary depends on several factors such as experience, skills, education, and budget. Salary range may vary based on geographic location.WHAT YOU'D BE DOING IN THIS ROLEOwn and manage projects across DECIEM’s full product development lifecycle, from R&D through to product launch. Lead and coordinate multiple complex projects at the same time, ensuring clear timelines, scope, budget, and delivery. Partner closely with cross-functional teams (Scientific, Production, Supply Chain, Brand, Commercial, Finance, L&D, etc.) to plan and execute product roadmaps. Act as the main point of contact for project-related questions, updates, risks, and changes, escalating where needed. Communicate project status, progress, and deviations clearly and consistently, including accurate updates in project tools (e.g. Wrike). Ensure all teams understand their responsibilities, deliverables, and deadlines, keeping projects aligned and on track. Support, guide, and upskill project managers and coordinators, ensuring a strong understanding of processes and ways of working. Design, build, and improve project processes, flows, and frameworks, including creating new ones from scratch when required. Drive continuous improvement by reviewing outcomes, documenting lessons learned, and strengthening project delivery standards. SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS NEEDED TO GET THE JOB DONEDegree or diploma in Project Management, Business, Science, Cosmetics, IT, or a related field; PMP or equivalent certification is a strong asset. 8+ years of hands-on project delivery experience, including product development and launch, with 4+ years in a leadership or managerial role. Proven experience managing complex projects in cosmetics or consumer packaged goods is highly valued. Strong business acumen with the ability to drive change, make sound decisions, and lead discussions with senior leadership. title: Project Manager (Health/Hospitals)
description: Infrastructure Ontario (IO), an Ontario crown corporation, provides a wide range of services to support the Ontario government’s initiative to modernize and maximize the value of public infrastructure and realty. IO has been recognized domestically and internationally for the way it does business and the success it has had with its major projects, real estate services, infrastructure lending, commercial projects, and development mandate. IO delivers some of the most complex, ambitious commercial and land development solutions for the Province and continues to evolve and expand its capabilities to maximize its public impact and deliver on its vision of creating a connected, modern and competitive Ontario.Project Delivery manages large, complex public infrastructure projects using a variety of contracting models, notably the public-private partnership (P3) approach to procurement and delivery. The P3 model leverages private sector financing and expertise in building public infrastructure with the objective of delivering projects on schedule and within approved budgets. The current project pipeline is valued at more than $35 billion in estimated design and construction costs.Job PurposeThe role is responsible for managing the procurement and/or implementation of major healthcare projects through Public-Private Partnerships (P3), or related approved contractual models.To be successful in this role, the incumbent will have sound stakeholder management skills and experience finding positive solutions to complex issues with clients and diverse teams of project consultants and specialists. Advanced presentation, consultation, negotiation, influencing and consensus building skills are required. Experience negotiating claims, scope changes and other compliance and contractual matters on major projects is preferred.What You Will Do Manage the procurement and implementation of major healthcare projects through Public-Private Partnerships (P3) and other approved contractual models.Manage relationships with various groups of internal and external stakeholders to identify issues related to staging, procurement, government approvals, relationship management and other commercial issues.Develop and implement risk identification, assessment and identifying mitigation measures, specific to commercial issues in any phase of the project.Manage projects from Pre-transaction, Request for Qualifications (RFQ) and Request for Proposal (RFP) stage during procurement and through construction, commissioning to the final transfer of responsibility to operations.Support the Director and Executives on the overall management of a major Project (or group of Projects) which includes providing commercial advice on contracts and developing commercial strategy for successful project execution. Provide commercial support and liaise with the technical team and consultants, as required, throughout the planning, procurement, design, and construction phases of a project.What We Are Looking ForSubstantial hands-on experience managing major, complex projects (Healthcare preferred) from Transaction through to Construction stages, including executing all the project activities from start to the end for the entire duration.Experience or education in Engineering, Project Management, Architecture or relevant discipline.PMP certification or in the process of acquiring.Experience with P3 and traditional delivery (CCDC2 or CCDC5B) programs, specifically in healthcare is an asset.Experience in contract administration is an asset.Strong experience managing major projects from inception to delivery is required.Strong business acumen, solid experience dealing with project budget, schedule, and technical requirements as well as financial and legal matters, contract management, with an emphasis on commercial considerations pertaining to project planning and implementation.Thorough knowledge of contract structuring and legal requirements of contracts.Experience leading and managing project teams to guide the direction of scope and manage ongoing project discussions with stakeholders.Experience guiding and coaching Project teams (Project Managers and Coordinators).Significant experience collaborating with internal and external stakeholders to understand their needs, requirements, and concerns related to the project.Experience leading meetings with senior or executive leadership regarding critical project outcomes for the portfolio.Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills to identify and assess potential risks and challenges associated with the project, programs, and developing strategies to mitigate them. What We OfferCommitment to creating a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture that promotes a sense of belonging and represents and reflects the needs of the communities we serve.A defined contribution pension plan, where IO contributes the equivalent of 5% of a full-time employee’s annual salary, with an option for IO to match an additional 5% contribution made by the employee. IO also offers a Group Retirement Savings Plan and Group Tax Free Savings Account, designed to help IO colleagues plan and save for their future.A comprehensive package that covers health, dental, vision, out-of-country care, paramedical services, and more.A pregnancy and parental leave program that offers expecting, eligible employees a top-up to 95% of their annual base salary for 31 weeks, and our parental leave program offers eligible employees a top-up to 95% of their annual base salary for 15 weeks.Access to our $1000 Healthcare Spending Account to top up expenses not covered in the benefits program and a Lifestyle Account that expands the eligibility of health and wellness options and can include coverage for gym memberships and fitness equipment, nutrition counselling or financial planning.Support for professional development opportunities for all colleagues through a broad range of learning programs that include in-person and online training, leadership development, and support for colleagues’ well-being.IO will also cover the costs associated with up to two membership and license fees per year for eligible colleagues if directly related to the employee’s role.Access to a suite of virtual healthcare options to support non-urgent medical needs.A convenient, central location at 1 Dundas St West, easily accessible by public transit.We offer a competitive salary range of $110,900 to $143,600 (PM3 level), pay will align with skills, knowledge and experience of the candidate.This posting will close on March 16, 2026 at 11:59 pm.Interviews will occur on a rolling basis until the role is filled.Exact compensation may vary based on skills, knowledge and experience.This posting is for a current active vacancy. We are actively recruiting to fill this position.After reviewing the qualifications listed above, we want you to know that we understand you may not meet all the qualifications described, but still encourage you to apply, as you may have other relevant expertise and experience that you can bring to the role.At Infrastructure Ontario, we strive to create a culture that encourages a diverse equitable and inclusive environment that fosters belonging. We believe that experience comes in many forms and through learning from each others’ differences and perspectives gives us strength. We are committed to enhancing our culture and encourage applications from all walks of life. Let us know if you require any accommodations for the selection process, by contacting the Talent Acquisition Team.Candidates who are members of Indigenous, Black, racialized and 2SLGBTQ+ communities, persons with disabilities, and other equity deserving groups are encouraged to apply, and their lived experience will be taken into consideration as applicable to the posted position.At Infrastructure Ontario, we use AI in our applicant tracking system to improve the efficiency and fairness of recruitment. Your application will be reviewed by both AI and our hiring team for a thorough evaluation. Please note that final hiring decisions are made solely by our experienced hiring team.

title: Project Coordinator, Northern Corridors (Barrie Line)
description: Job DescriptionMetrolinx is connecting communities across the Greater Golden Horseshoe. Metrolinx operates GO Transit and UP Express, as well as the PRESTO fare payment system. We are also building new and improved rapid transit, including GO Expansion, Light Rail Transit routes, and major expansions to Toronto’s subway system, to get people where they need to go, better, faster and easier. Metrolinx is an agency of the Government of Ontario.At Metrolinx, equity, diversity and inclusion are essential to living our values of serving with passion, thinking forward and playing as a team.CPG’s workforce is made up of driven and dedicated professionals who care deeply about working together to deliver infrastructure and make a positive impact in our communities. Join us as we build a knowledgeable and collaborative team that courageously changes how transit projects will be delivered.Start Rate (Non-negotiable): $49.68 per hourThe Norther Corridors Barrie Team is seeking a Project Coordinator who, reporting to the Manager, will coordinate with Internal and External Stakeholders to complete the design/construction/relocation of the assigned transit infrastructure projects as part of the Capital Projects Group in a safe, timely, consistent, and cost-effective manner.What will I be doing?Oversee the project work of design and construction to ensure compliance with the Project Agreement, Metrolinx’s Engineering Standards, Building Code and Site Plan Control Requirements and internal user group requirements through all project lifecycle stages.Coordinate reviews of project contractor’s site work plans and ground disturbance permits to minimize customer and Metrolinx revenue service impacts, ensure all internal and external approvals from stakeholders are obtained, and that reviews are completed timely and schedule restrictions are adhered to. Oversee all site works and construction works, working directly with contractors on site, through coordination from the office, as well as site visits.Plan, track and report all capital project expenditures such as consultant fees and addenda, purchase orders, construction contracts, change orders.Work with Project Manager to manage risk, cost, schedule, permits and all other performance indicators related to execution of the work.Will be working in a fast-paced schedule construction environment and will need to be able to problem solve and work collaboratively with others to resolve issues and facilitate the execution of the works.Prepare and submit tender document specifications for construction and consultant contracts for submission to the Procurement Team for tendering. Assists Procurement Team during tendering by providing information to contractors and consultants.Coordinate projects to minimize customer impacts, ensure all internal and external approvals are obtained, and that design and cost dispute resolution and timely completion within established budget and schedule restrictions are adhered to.Participation with on site construction meetings, site reviews, and safety compliance, including ensuring compliance with contract document management.Participate in ongoing risk reviews and schedule management. Identify and document potential risk impacts. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to provide input into the development and implementation of risk mitigation plans. Coordinate and maintain commercial issues, change variations, and potential claims.Ensure project completion and commissioning for handover to user group. Prepare reports, logs, and presentations to various stakeholders and the management team on a regular basis, working collaboratively to meet internal and external stakeholder requirements.What Skills and Qualifications Do I Need?A college diploma or degree in Civil Engineering Technology or an equivalent combination of education, training and experience providing a thorough knowledge of the theories and practices of engineering/architecture. Minimum five (5) years’ experience related to the planning, design and construction of transit facilities, interpreting and applying contract plans and specifications and co-ordinating contracted engineering projects. Knowledge of the theory, principles and practices of civil engineering and architecture. Detailed knowledge of project management including cost control, scheduling, codes, standards, specifications, corporate procedures and applicable contract legislation. Understanding of Railway Rules and Regulations and Safety & Operating procedures. Excellent communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills, with the ability to work independently and think analytically.Proficiency in the use of project management software and other applications such as Bluebeam, Revit, GIS, MS Excel& AutoCAD would be desirable.Site visits may involve exposure to different levels of construction at stations, parking lots, platforms, and railway corridors along with climbing ladders and scaffolding and exposure to inclement weather.This position requires the successful candidate to be present at various designated onsite locations as assigned by their leader, if required. Additionally, as an operational business continuity functional coordinator, the candidate should be prepared to make visits during evenings, weekends, and holidays as necessary.Experience conducting regular site visits, documenting progress, identifying issues, and preparing clear, accurate site observation reports.Ability to interpret construction drawings, specifications, and schedules to support site observations and project reporting.Experience coordinating with contractors, consultants, inspectors, and internal stakeholders to track actions, deficiencies, and follow-ups.Have a valid Ontario “G” Driver’s Licence, in good standing for regular site visits. Driver’s Abstract (within the last 3 Years) will be required of the successful candidate.AccommodationWe value the unique skills and experiences each person brings to Metrolinx and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment. We are committed to the requirements of the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act so if you require accommodation during the hiring process, please let our Recruitment team know by contacting us at: 416-202-5601 or email hr.recruitment@metrolinx.com.Application ProcessAll applicants must be legally entitled to work in Canada. Metrolinx will be using email to communicate with you for all job competitions. It is your responsibility to include an updated email address that is checked daily and accepts emails from unknown users. As we send time-sensitive correspondence, we recommend that you check your email regularly. If no response is received, we will assume you are no longer interested in pursuing the opportunity. Please be advised that a Criminal Record Check may be required of the successful candidate.Should it be determined that any background information provided is misleading, inaccurate or incorrect, Metrolinx reserves the right to discontinue with the consideration of your application.We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.WE ARE AN EQUITABLE AND INCLUSIVE EMPLOYER.

title: Project Manager, Operations (12 - Month Contract)
description: About Nicola Wealth:We have a lot to be proud of at Nicola Wealth. Our sophisticated financial planning, our diversified private investment funds and their exceptional performance, our 98% client retention rate and our award-winning culture, combine to position us as a national leader in wealth management. Recognized as a Best Managed Company and one of Canada’s Top Employers, we are dedicated to upholding our firm’s values through this phase of significant growth.By bringing together smart, dedicated and supportive colleagues from diverse backgrounds, we deliver extraordinary client service in a values-driven work environment. The collaboration, creativity and entrepreneurial spirit we were founded on provides the opportunity for you to make a real impact and build a rewarding career. We are passionate about our business, our culture and investing deeply in your growth and development to build for the future. If you have this same drive, then let’s talk!Purpose of the Role::Reporting to the Director, Operations, the Project Manager, Operations will join the team on a 12-month contract dedicated to the SimCorp Dimension project. This role focuses on business consultation and project management to support operational initiatives to drive the continuous improvement and scalability of our business. This role requires broad stakeholder engagement and project management experience and the ability to lead projects from concept to completion.Core Responsibilities:Lead and support workstreams involved in SimCorp Dimension configuration, testing, data migration, change management and deployment. Work closely with SimCorp, external consultants, and internal stakeholders to coordinate tasks, track progress, and resolve issues. Facilitate defect triage, testing, and cross-workstream dependency management Deliver using appropriate methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid), ensuring alignment with strategic goals and timely execution. Collaborate with the Technology team, SimCorp, and external consultants to successfully guide a complex, technology-focused project by drawing on previous experience with large-scale or technical initiatives to enable efficient coordination and completion. Demonstrate flexibility and adaptability in managing both large-scale initiatives and smaller, fast-moving projects, adjusting approach and tools as needed. Manage and report on project activities, including risks, issues, and decisions. Drive conversations with Executive Sponsors to clarify priorities, obtain time from subject matter experts (SMEs), and validate recommendations. Work with Project Sponsors to document and confirm project objectives, benefits, and inter-dependencies. Facilitate effective communication across teams, ensuring transparency, alignment, and timely resolution of issues or blockers. Conduct hands-on process and business analysis, including workflow mapping, data analysis, and root cause investigation to support informed decision-making and continuous improvement. Collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders to gather requirements, define project goals, and translate business needs into actionable plans. Champion operational excellence, identifying opportunities for efficiency and scalability within existing workflows. Monitor and report on project performance, using metrics and insights to drive accountability and optimize outcomes, and quantify the benefits of implemented changes. Support change management by obtaining agreement from leadership and business owners to implement new processes, tools or systems, and tracking the impact of those changes. Required Skills & Qualifications:Minimum of 3 – 5 years of project management experience or equivalent, preferably in the financial services industry. Bachelor’s Degree in a related discipline. Project Management education and/or certifications are an asset. Solid understanding of the wealth management industry, financial markets, financial planning, asset management, and/or insurance. Experience working on SimCorp Dimension is an asset. Experience with project management/delivery software. Highly proficient with word processing, spreadsheet, and presentation software. Exposure to change management and/or process optimization is an asset. Experience collaborating with vendor partners and external consultants in complex technology implementations. Compensation & Benefits Package:This position comes with a competitive base salary hiring range of $80,000 - $100,000 dependent on experience, skills, qualifications and internal equity.At Nicola Wealth, our people grow our business, delight our clients, and live our values every day. Our comprehensive compensation and benefits package reflects our appreciation for this commitment. Whether it’s participating in our profit sharing and benefit programs on day one, or the opportunity to participate in our share ownership program, our people are able to go beyond a conventional salary and bonus structure. To learn more about our complete package, visit: www.nicolawealth.com/total-rewards.This role is a current vacancy within Nicola Wealth.Please include both a cover letter and resume in support of your application. We are an inclusive, equal opportunity employer. For more information about this and other roles: www.nicolawealth.com/careers.It is our priority to remove barriers to provide equal access to employment. Should you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process please contact us at accessibility@nicolawealth.com and we will work with you to meet your needs. All information shared during the accommodation request process will be stored and used in a manner that is consistent with applicable laws and Nicola Wealth policies.Thank you for your interest in a career with Nicola Wealth!

drawings, purchase orders, contracts, building permits, change order requests and pricing, RFIs, RFQs, close out etc.Setup and maintain accurate project information and data in our computerized BRINGPost-secondary education in Construction Management, Architecture, Engineering, or a related field.3+ years of project coordination experience at a general contractor, ideally on complex
