title: Senior Scrum Master (Technical) - POS
description: Position OverviewThe Senior Scrum Master is a strategic role accountable for the successful, on-time delivery of complex technology solutions across multiple teams within a scaled Agile program.This role requires a dedicated servant leader who integrates advanced Agile facilitation, including leading Scrum of Scrums, with robust program management principles. The position includes all standard Scrum Master responsibilities along with the necessary skills for long-term planning and  managing project delivery.You will act as the primary strategic liaison, building strong relationships with business and technology stakeholders to ensure clear alignment and promote a continuous improvement culture. This position is central to the program's success, requiring clear, transparent reporting on team progress, velocity, and program financials to drive decision-making. Success demands strong mentorship skills and a focus on removing large-scale obstacles to ensure maximum delivery value.Lead Scrum Ceremonies Facilitate all Scrum ceremonies (Sprint Planning, Daily Standups, Sprint Reviews/Demos, Retrospectives, and Backlog Refinements) ensuring they are effective and adhere to the timebox.   Proactively identify and remove impediments to help teams achieve sprint goals, coordinating with stakeholders and other teams as needed.   Lead and facilitate Scrum of Scrums  Develop a solid understanding of the product and underlying technologies to support informed decision-making and effective delivery. Project and Program Management Delivery Develop project plans, set milestones, and manage deadlines.   Monitor progress, assess risks, implement mitigation strategies, and adjust schedules as needed.   Act as the primary point of contact for stakeholders, ensuring alignment with business objectives and adherence to quality standards.   Apply DMO best practices and maintain required artifacts throughout the delivery lifecycle.  Define program strategy, objectives, and success metrics in partnership with Technology leadership and key stakeholders.   Engage with stakeholders on regular basis to ensure alignment and provide clear, timely updates on program status. Stakeholder management Strategically manage, align, and coordinate a diverse set of stakeholders across multiple business and technology to resolve complex, program-wide dependencies and drive clear communication channels across large-scale initiatives.   Foster and support multiple business and technology teams by identifying and removing obstacles, resolving large complex impediments and dependencies.   Promote a culture of continuous improvement within the teams and help the teams implement changes.   Observe and understand factors that impact team performance and velocity with the goal of continuously improving and applying learnings to future sprints.  Collaborate with Business Product Owners and the Tech delivery teams to ensure each sprint delivers the defined sprint scope.   Assist in managing the product backlog, clarify backlog items, and facilitate effective communication between the Product Owner and the team Leadership ·Support and facilitate interaction within the team and with key members from Business and other Technology Teams such as Change Management, Infrastructure, Security, Architecture, DBAs, and Offshore Support Team Members.  Participate in organistion's agile community meetups to contribute and learn about potential improvements to our agile best practices and delivery approach and ensures that the team follows Scrum practices and principles.   Help the team to understand the theory, practices, rules, and values of the Scrum framework.   Coach and mentor others on Agile principles and practices.   Drive alignment across teams by partnering with Scrum Masters, Product Owners, and stakeholders to enable seamless cross-functional collaboration. Product/Business Knowledge Report on key metrics such as progress, velocity, financials, risks, and issues to ensure stakeholders and the Agile DMO Group have clear visibility.  Identify and present the most relevant metrics for Business and Tech stakeholders to support their decision-making processes.   Manage the complexities of large-scale programs by maintaining a comprehensive understanding of the product.   Drive innovation and continuous improvements by identifying opportunities for optimization and implementing scalable solutions across teams.   Lead and support technical delivery by applying a strong understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC), engineering best practices such, and facilitating effective technical discussions between stakeholders. RequirementsQualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.   7+ years of experience as a Scrum Master, with at least 2 years in a technical environment.   Previous experience in POS and retail is strong asset.   Proven track record of delivering successful software projects in Agile environments.   Solid understanding of software development processes, technical principles and processes.   Exposure with modern technologies, such as cloud computing, microservices, DevOps practices, or APIs, is a plus.   Ability to communicate effectively and collaborate with technical teams.   Certifications (preferred):   Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Professional Scrum Master (PSM), or equivalent.   SAFe, PMI-ACP, or other Agile-related certifications are a plus.  Skills and Competencies  Expert knowledge of the Agile Principles, Kanban and Scrum framework.   Strong servant leadership, communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills.  Excellent facilitation, coaching, and mentoring skills.   Proficient documentation skills.   Ability to identify and remove impediments effectively.  Proven ability to manage and prioritize multiple parallel initiatives in fast-paced, dynamic environments.   Advanced project / program management expertise, including planning, execution, risk mitigation, and stakeholder alignment.   Effective presentation skills, with the ability to convey complex information to diverse audiences.  Strong stakeholder and vendor management skills Benefits Work with a passionate and innovative team committed to delivering high-quality solutions.   Opportunity to contribute your technical expertise to impactful projects.   A collaborative and inclusive work culture that values continuous learning and growth.   Hybrid work option  Extended healthcare

title: Scrum Master
description: Ready to Transform Retail? Embark on a rewarding career with Sobeys Inc., celebrated among Canada’s Top 100 employers where your unique contributions drive success.Sobeys is full of exciting opportunities, and we are always looking for bright new talent to join our team! We currently have an full time opportunity for an Scrum Master to join the Platform team. This role can be based out of our office in either in Stellarton, NS; Mississauga, ON; Calgary, AB.You are an influencer and change agent.In this exciting role, you will be facilitating communication between cross functional IT teams to avoid delays. In some cases, this may involve calls to resolve any internal or external conflicts involving software vendors and IT groups.You can effectively undertake the application lifecycle which consists of coordinating the integration, security, testing requests, and timelines across product deliverables. This involves supporting and aligning product workstreams, continually improving processes and reporting as well as providing ongoing support to the solutions manager and technical lead. In addition, you can successfully manage backlog and maintenance items into sprints for on-time deployment. You will be the go-to-person to help remove backlog blockers and to ensure vendors, users, and product teams are informed and aligned.Join Us And You’ll Be Working With Leading tools and systems in the exciting areas of Loyalty Strong, collaborative team of energetic and experienced digital expertsResponsibilities Agile practices: Understand the ecosystem and practically apply relevant agile practices to the backlog management process. Guide the larger team on Scrum principles, practices, and ceremonies to enhance productivity and delivery. Backlog management: Facilitate the end-to-end backlog process, including gathering requirements, prioritization, planning, and delivery. Work closely with solution managers, analysts, and stakeholders to ensure a clear understanding of scope, priorities, and timely delivery of enhancements. Managing a complex environment: Leverage your experience in working with complex, integrated ecosystems to navigate and resolve challenges that may arise during product development and delivery. Be the primary point of contact for enhancement-related communication, updates, and dependencies. Collaboration with product teams: Collaborate with multiple product teams, and business stakeholders to deliver enhancements. This can involve ensuring cross-team dependencies, testing and integrations are managed effectively. Incident management: Assist in incident management by coordinating with relevant teams, resolving issues, and ensuring incidents are handled efficiently and effectively. Continuous improvement: Identify areas for improvement in the enhancement and incident management processes. Encourage the team to continuously improve their practices to enhance efficiency and delivery. Risk management: Proactively identify risks and dependencies related to enhancement delivery and work with the team and stakeholders to mitigate potential issues or escalate as necessary. Adaptability - Be flexible and adaptive, knowing when to apply agile practices and when to use traditional project management techniques. Supports an environment where team freely escalates challenges; facilitates change through positive reinforcement of successes. Contribute to skills and capabilities development of the team through mentorship, sharing knowledge and best practises. Demonstrated ability to work effectively with minimal supervision and in a team environmentRequiredWhat you have to offer: Proven experience as a Scrum Master / Project Manager in IT projects. Strong understanding and practical application of Agile principles and Scrum framework. Familiarity with enhancement and incident management processes. Proficiency in AgilePlace / JIRA or similar user story and ticket management tools. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to facilitate collaboration and conflict resolution. Knowledge of best practices for product development and delivery processes.Preferred Experience working in a complex and integrated ecosystem, collaborating with multiple product teams. Experience in working with outsourced service providers and vendor management. Information technology background, professional certification (CSM, KMP etc.) or equivalent. Experience and/or expertise in application development, support (Level 2 & 3), delivery and deployment in retail industry.Who We AreWe started in a small town in Nova Scotia but we are now in communities of all sizes across this great country. With over 1,600 stores in all 10 provinces, you may know us as Sobeys, Safeway, IGA, Foodland, FreshCo, Thrifty Foods, Lawtons Drug Stores or another of our great banners but we are all one extended family.Our family of 128,000 employees and franchise affiliates share a collective passion for delivering exceptional shopping experiences and amazing food to all our customers. Our mission is to nurture the things that make life better – great experiences, families, communities, and our employees. We are a family nurturing families.Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion (DE&I) is fuelled by our purpose and values. It shapes our culture and drives business success. As a family nurturing families, we embed DE&I into everything we do. We know that it takes open minds and respect for distinct perspectives to create engaging workplaces, inclusive customer experiences and strong community partnerships. We are committed to accommodating applicants with disabilities throughout the hiring process and will work with applicants requesting accommodation at any stage of this process.What We OfferOur Total Rewards package is designed to help our teammates thrive—physically, financially, and emotionally. title: Scrum Master
description: Requisition ID: 252624Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.Scotia Tech is the engineering and technology engine of Scotiabank. We design, build, and operate the digital platforms, applications, and infrastructure that power the Bank globally. Our teams deliver secure, scalable, and resilient solutions that make it easier for our customers and business partners to interact with us every day.Guided by a clear strategic framework, Scotia Tech focuses on delivering engineering excellence, simplifying our technology ecosystem, adopting modern tools, and strengthening cybersecurity to reduce risk and improve stability. Our work enables the Bank to innovate faster, enhance customer experiences, and build a future‑ready technology foundation.PurposeWe are looking for a driven and passionate Scrum Master to champion agile methodologies and build a culture of accountability and continuous improvement with our Global Payments Engineering team. You will work closely with engineering, product, design, and delivery teams to facilitate agile ceremonies, remove impediments, and ensure alignment around sprint goals and priorities. Your efforts will directly impact the way we deliver value to our customers and enable high-performing teams to thrive.What You’ll DoBe an empowering leader and enable your teams to be highly functioning, self-organizing, and continuously improving Facilitate key agile ceremonies including daily standups, sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives Partner with Product Owners, Engineering Leads, and other stakeholders to define sprint goals, refine backlogs, and deliver on roadmaps Identify and remove blockers or obstacles that impact team productivity and delivery Oversee and manage program releases and implementations Foster transparency and collaboration across teams and stakeholders Support agile adoption and coach team members on Scrum practices and principles Track and report team progress through agile metrics and tools, and use data to drive continuous improvement Create a safe and inclusive team environment, encouraging experimentation, innovation, and open feedback Serves as a steward of team culture, environment, and engineering practices Comfortable to both encourage and challenge others to align behaviors towards our mindset Influence, question, and evaluate processes to determine opportunities for effectiveness Ability to come to office at least 4 days a week Act as a steward of agile values, practices, and team health Comfortable with challenging the status quo and helping others evolve mindsets and behaviors Thrive in a fast-moving, team-based environment where experimentation is encouraged Passionate about empowering others and facilitating alignment toward shared goals What You'll Bring5+ years of IT experience in the related field3+ years of People Management experience and/or leading large cross-functional/cross-regional teams on major organizational projects, preferably in an agile work environmentDesire and drive for innovation through problem solving, leveraging resourcefulness as a key skill; have a learning mindset while intrinsically motivated to learn new tools and techniques Intermediate to advanced level of experience as a Scrum Master or Agile Coach Deep understanding of Agile frameworks, especially Scrum and Kanban Strong facilitation skills with a proven ability to lead productive team ceremonies and retrospectives Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build relationships and influence without authority Experience working with cross-functional teams including engineering, product, and design Proficiency in agile tools such as Jira, Confluence, or similar Certified Scrum Master (CSM), SAFe, or equivalent agile certification is an asset Comfortable with continuously changing environments (examples: business priorities, new technologies) Exhibit a high degree of understanding risk management Leverage and understanding of the use of analytics to enable day-to-day conversations and decision making Working ConditionsWork in a standard office-based environment.Interested? That’s why we work to grow and diversify talent and engage employees in a performance-oriented culture.What's in it for you? Inclusive and collaborative working environment which encourages creativity and celebrates success.Opportunities to work with and learn from industry leaders.Access to professional development resources and training programs.Competitive compensation and benefits package, including performance bonuses and health coverage.Scotiabank wants you to be able to bring your best self to work – and life, every day. With a focus on holistic well-being, our many flexible benefit programs are designed to help support your unique family, financial, physical, mental, and social health needs.Location(s): Canada : Ontario : TorontoScotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. title: Senior Project Manager, Data Center Construction
description: Req Id: 428428Connection is everything. It drives us to innovate, explore, and stay close to what matters to us most. At Bell, we’re building a more connected future through world-class networks, AI-powered solutions, and digital experiences that elevate how people live, work, and play every day.We believe in empowering people. That’s why we equip our teams with cutting-edge technology, AI tools, and a collaborative environment that supports creativity and growth. Want to be part of a diverse team where your work makes a real impact? If you’re inspired by innovation that advances how people connect and transforms what’s possible, you belong on #TeamBell.At Bell, data science experts like you are shaping the future. You'll create, apply and work with algorithms that learn and analyze data, just like the human brain. By leveraging AI and machine learning technologies, you'll help us deliver better experiences and service innovations to Canadians.Plus, through our partnerships, you'll have the opportunity to learn and grow alongside other AI professionals to advance your career and make a lasting impact.SummaryBell Canada is seeking a hands-on Data Centre Construction Manager to lead end-to-end delivery of new Data Centre builds and upgrades. You will coordinate partners and contractors to drive scope, schedule, budget, safety, and quality, ensuring on-time delivery. After commissioning and handover, the role transitions to Data Centre Manager, leading a local operations team and partnering closely with Facilities to maintain high availability, reliability, and compliance for mission-critical services. This is a customer-facing role that requires exceptional interpersonal skills and the ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders.Key ResponsibilitiesConstruction / Build PhaseOwn end-to-end delivery of greenfield and brownfield Data Centre projects from design development through commissioning and handover.Plan and manage scope, schedule, budget, risk, quality, and EHS for construction activities while maintaining integrated master schedules and the critical path.Lead day‑to‑day coordination with general contractors, trades, OEMs, and consultants by running site meetings, and supporting change control.Drive readiness for high‑density AI and HPC deployments, including power, cooling using air and or liquid solutions, and structured cabling.Support procurement, contract administration, and cost‑control activities; assist with validating progress draws and claims and help track contingencies and value‑engineering opportunities.Report progress, risks, and mitigations to leadership and stakeholders with clear status dashboards.Transition to Operations & Steady State ManagementLead and manage the local Data Centre operations team post‑build, ensuring effective team performance, operational readiness, and alignment with availability and reliability objectives.Partner with Facilities to ensure availability and reliability targets are met for all critical systems, including power, cooling, fire protection, and security.Support preventive and corrective maintenance activities with OEMs and service providers by reviewing and approving MOP, SOP, and EOP activities through established change‑control processes.Monitor capacity across power, cooling, and space and plan expansions while coordinating customer moves, adds, and changes and commissioning new loads.Drive continuous improvement in safety, operations, and energy efficiency, including PUE, by leveraging DCIM and other tools and operational KPIs.Support customer onboarding by contributing to design coordination and technical alignment, supporting quoting and scope definition, coordinating power, cooling, and connectivity readiness, and guiding clients through implementation milestones to ensure smooth deployment of new workloads.Serve as a customer‑facing leader for site tours, readiness reviews, incident communications, and service improvement initiatives.Maintain compliance with corporate policies, security requirements, and regulatory standards while leading audits and remediation activities.Critical QualificationsBachelor’s degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Business, Information Technology, or a related technical or operational field, or an equivalent combination of education and experience in mission‑critical facilities or Data Centre operations. Minimum of 4–8 years of progressive experience in construction project management, ideally within Data Centres or mission-critical facilities.Demonstrated experience leading and managing operational or project teams, including performance management, coaching, and day‑to‑day team leadership.Deep understanding of Data Centre design and construction methodologies, including power, cooling, fire suppression, and cabling.Experience with contract negotiation, administration, and vendor management across GC, trades, and OEMs.Familiarity with applicable building codes, OHS/safety legislation, and environmental regulations.PMP (or equivalent) is highly desirable, familiar with MS Project and standard productivity tools.Preferred QualificationsExceptional leadership, interpersonal, and communication skills with a strong customer focus.Ability to influence diverse stakeholders and navigate complex partner relationships.Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; proactive risk management and decision-making.Experience operating mission-critical facilities and collaborating with Facilities/MEP teams.Familiarity with DCIM/CMMS platforms and site KPI management; AI/HPC/GPU deployments a plus.Bilingual (French/English) is an asset.Adequate knowledge of French is required for positions in Quebec.Additional InformationPosition Type: ManagementJob Status: Regular - Full TimeJob Location: Canada : Ontario : Toronto || Canada : British Columbia : Kamloops || Canada : Manitoba : Winnipeg || Canada : Ontario : Mississauga || Canada : Saskatchewan : Regina || Canada : Saskatchewan : SaskatoonWork Arrangement: HybridApplication Deadline: 03/16/2026For work arrangements that are ‘Hybrid’, successful candidates must be based in Canada and report to a set Bell office for a minimum of 3 days a week. Recognizing the importance of work-life balance, Bell offers flexibility in work hours based on the business needs.Please apply directly online to be considered for this role. Applications through email will not be accepted.We know that caring for our team members is at the heart of a healthy, positive and thriving workplace. As part of our team, you’ll enjoy a comprehensive compensation package that includes a competitive salary and a wide range of benefits to support the well-being of you and your family. As soon as you join us, you'll be eligible for medical, dental, vision and mental health benefits that you can tailor to your specific needs. Plus, as a Bell team member, you'll enjoy a 35% discount on our services and access exclusive offers from our partners.At Bell, we are proud of our focus on fostering an inclusive and accessible workplace where all team members feel valued, respected, supported, and that they belong.Bell is committed to clarity in our hiring process. All roles posted are opportunities we’re actively recruiting for, unless stated otherwise. We also want to make sure that everyone has an equal opportunity to join our team. We encourage individuals who may require accommodations during the hiring process to let us know. For a confidential inquiry, email your recruiter or recruitment@bell.ca to make arrangements. If you have questions or feedback regarding accessibility at Bell, we invite you to complete the Accessibility feedback form or visit our Accessibility page for other ways to contact us.Artificial intelligence may be used to assess parts of your application. Please review our privacy policy (see Phenom for details) to learn more about how we collect, use, and disclose your personal information.Created: Canada, ON, TorontoBell, one of Canada's Top 100 Employers.

title: Senior Project Manager
description: WHY DO WE NEED YOU?We have an exciting opportunity for a high-visibility project as a Senior Project Manager based in Calgary, Alberta or the GTA, Ontario, to join our Corporate Engineering team. This is a great chance to join a Top Employer and contribute to our growth and success.The Senior Project Manager will provide exceptional leadership with clear direction and accountability for major capital projects. Handling the full scope of the installation, from initial budget requests and cost estimating through phased engineering, procurement, construction, and commissioning, to ensure timely completion, our group EHS standards are paramount at every stage of the project.We evolve in a World Class Manufacturing (Continuous Improvement program) environment, utilizing established Project Management Standards. In this role, you will be responsible for several large projects in unison, completed with the highest regard for safety, environment, quality and reliability, as well as performance ethics.The project will focus on the new build, implementation of energy, controls, technology, and infrastructure capital improvements. Projects will vary from multiple contracts to oversight of turnkey installations.IS THIS JOB FOR YOU?Essential DutiesCompletes phased engineering studies of projects for North American facilities, developing and evaluating conceptual alternatives in support of business needs.Prepares Capex authorization requests and presents to Senior Management for approval.Develops and maintains sound business relationships with Engineering’s Customers, Vendors, Suppliers, and contract resources and ensures alignment with our needs and expectations.Optimal use of competitive bidding, Low-Cost Country (LCC) sourcing techniques to designated countries for competitive installation costs.Provides Project Execution Plans, including Critical Path Schedules, Resource Plans, Project Risk Registers, and Budget and Variance reports.Strict regard for Project Scope and Change Management procedures.Utilizes World Class Manufacturing (WCM) and Early Equipment Management tools for new designs and installations.Collaborates with global internal and external project management experts to assure Best Practice implementation within our facilities.Directs project engineers; coordinates external vendors, installation contractors.Trains and leads the project management community in the identification and application of efficient project management tools and techniques.Key Contributor in assuring the company’s immediate and long-term capital program objectives are achieved.Assures capital program is implemented cost-effectively. Prepares capital cost estimates, capex budget requests, project spending variance reports, purchase orders, as well as invoice approvals. Assures projects achieve business benefit before closure. Join us and be part of a future where innovation meets sustainability!Benefits In Joining UsWe provide unique options to fit your unique lives! Our Total Rewards Program offers a total approach to well-being!Our amazing benefits and programs include, but are not limited to:Excellent healthcare options: Medical, vision, prescription & dentalFamily Focus & Balance: Parental leave, paid time-off, and Employee Assistance ProgramFinancial Security: Company-funded Retirement Accumulation Plan - Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit-Sharing Plan (DPSP), Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA) and Employee Stock Purchase Program (PEG)Tuition Reimbursement: Continuing education for every season of your careerEmployee Recognition ProgramsLiveWell: Rewarding you for living a healthy lifestyleBe Well Be You: Our comprehensive benefits, resources, and programs platform to help you and your family be well, wherever you are in life (Emotional, Physical, Financial, and Social)Pet insurance options: Insurance plan & prescription discount program for your furry friendsPerks & Savings Program: access to thousands of gift cards, in-store and online discounts, as well as cashback offers.

title: Senior Project Manager / Scrum Master – Supply Chain & Merchandising
description: Senior Project Manager / Scrum Master – Supply Chain & Merchandising (Remote) Technology at *** Our technology organization delivers scalable systems, innovative digital retail experiences, and high‑impact business solutions. You will support key Supply Chain and Merchandising initiatives across Warehouse Management Systems (WMS), Inventory Planning, and Merchandising platforms.  Your role at *** As a Senior Project Manager / Scrum Master, you will drive Agile execution across three core tracks—WMS, Inventory Planning, and Merchandising—while partnering closely with engineering, product, supply chain operations, and cross‑functional business partners. You will facilitate all Agile ceremonies, lead the monthly Sprint Scope Review, and provide end‑to‑end program delivery support.  Responsibilities • Serve as Scrum Master for WMS, Inventory Planning, and Merchandising tracks. • Facilitate Agile ceremonies: sprint planning, daily stand‑ups, backlog refinement, sprint reviews, and retrospectives. • Lead the monthly Sprint Scope Review to align scope, risks, capacity, and priorities. • Develop delivery plans, track milestones, manage dependencies, and communicate status across stakeholders. • Partner with engineering, architecture, Client, product teams, and vendors to ensure aligned delivery. • Manage risks, issues, decisions, and scope in Agile and hybrid environments. • Produce high‑quality documentation: meeting summaries, action logs, RAID items, and communications. • Support additional supply chain and merchandising systems initiatives as needed.  We’re excited about you if you have • 7+ years of project management experience, including Agile/Scrum delivery. • Experience leading multiple Agile workstreams concurrently. • Background in Supply Chain, Merchandising, Retail Systems, or Enterprise Operations. • Strong communication, facilitation, negotiation, and stakeholder‑management skills. • Experience working in matrixed organizations with cross‑functional teams. • Must be proficient with Jira and Confluence • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience. • Preferred Certifications: Certified ScrumMaster (CSM) + one additional Agile certification (SAFe Agilist, PMI‑ACP, PSM, etc.). • Ability to work effectively during Pacific Time Zone (PT) hours.  You’ll love working here because • The people: Collaborative, supportive colleagues who value innovation and teamwork. • The business: *** leads experiential retail with a strong digital and client‑centric focus. • The LVMH family: Access to global brands and professional growth opportunities.
sector: Retail Apparel and Fashion, Retail, and Retail Luxury Goods and Jewelry

title: Scrum Master Senior
description: Scrum MasterÀ propos de Highspring Highspring est une firme de services?conseils spécialisée en transformation numérique, données et technologies. Ce rôle hybride (2 jours/semaine au bureau) implique de contribuer activement à la mise en place d'un environnement Agile performant, collaboratif et centré sur la valeur. Vous travaillerez de près avec les Product Owners, les équipes techniques et d'autres Scrum Masters pour soutenir la livraison de solutions numériques complexes. Why Join HighspringAt Highspring, you'll join a community of curious, collaborative, and impact?driven professionals. You'll contribute to meaningful digital initiatives while expanding your expertise alongside experienced consultants. We emphasize autonomy, personal growth, and a supportive environment where you can do your best work.

title: Sr. Scrum Master (SAP/Finance)
description: Job DescriptionAbout the Job: We are seeking a Senior Scrum Master with strong experience supporting SAP and Finance transformation initiatives to lead Agile delivery within complex enterprise environments. This role combines servant leadership, program delivery oversight, and financial systems governance to ensure reliable, compliant, and high-quality releases across SAP and finance platforms.The ideal candidate will facilitate Agile excellence while supporting cross-functional teams working on SAP modules, finance systems, integrations, and enterprise data platforms. The role will partner closely with Finance stakeholders, SAP functional teams, architects, and enterprise technology teams to deliver scalable financial solutions aligned with business priorities and regulatory requirements. Office Location: TorontoEmployment Type: PermanentRole Type: New position – current requirementWork Arrangement: Hybrid (2 days in office per week) Position Responsibilities:Agile & Scrum LeadershipFacilitate all Scrum ceremonies including Sprint Planning, Daily Standups, Reviews/Demos, Retrospectives, and Backlog Refinement.Drive Agile best practices across teams delivering SAP and Finance transformation initiatives.Lead Scrum of Scrums sessions to coordinate dependencies between SAP functional teams, integration teams, finance stakeholders, and data teams.Proactively identify and remove impediments impacting SAP releases, financial reporting cycles, and system integrations.Ensure sprint commitments align with business finance priorities, compliance needs, and regulatory deadlines.Promote Agile maturity and foster a culture of continuous improvement and transparency.SAP & Finance Delivery OversightCoordinate development and delivery across SAP functional consultants, technical developers, integration teams, and finance SMEs.Facilitate backlog grooming to ensure user stories reflect finance processes, accounting rules, and compliance requirements.Ensure appropriate validation and testing of financial workflows, reporting logic, and data accuracy.Support integration initiatives between SAP, ERP systems, data warehouses, and downstream financial applications.Monitor release readiness related to month-end, quarter-end, and year-end financial cycles.Program & Delivery ManagementDevelop and maintain project plans, release roadmaps, and quality milestones.Manage cross-team dependencies involving SAP, Finance ,OperationsMonitor velocity, sprint predictability, defect trends, and financial tracking at program level.Implement risk mitigation strategies for high-impact releases.Apply SDLC governance and best practices, and ensure required artifacts are maintained.Stakeholder & Cross-Functional ManagementServe as the primary liaison between Finance business leaders, Product Owners, SAP teams, Architecture, and IT leadership.Facilitate alignment between business finance priorities and technology delivery timelines.Provide transparent reporting on delivery progress, risks, and release readiness.Manage and resolve cross-team dependencies affecting finance transformation initiatives.Support financial governance initiatives including audit readiness, compliance reviews, and regulatory requirements.Leadership & CoachingMentor teams on Agile best practices within enterprise SAP and finance environments.Coach Product Owners and Finance stakeholders on Agile backlog management and prioritization strategies.Facilitate retrospectives and root cause analysis to improve delivery performance.Contribute to Agile communities of practice and help evolve delivery standards for SAP programs.RequirementsRequired Qualifications:·       Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Finance, or related field.·       8+ years of experience as a Scrum Master working within Agile environments.·       Strong understanding of enterprise SDLC, Agile frameworks, and release management practices.·       Experience managing delivery for SAP, or Finance transformation initiatives.·       Proven ability to coordinate cross-functional teams across business and technology domains.·       Experience managing complex dependencies in large-scale enterprise programs.·       Excellent communication, facilitation, and stakeholder management skills.·       Strong servant leadership mindset with proven ability to resolve delivery impediments. Preferred Qualifications:·       Experience working with SAP S/4HANA or core SAP Finance modules (FI/CO, AR/AP, GL).·       Exposure to financial reporting, accounting processes, and regulatory compliance requirements.·       Experience working in banking, financial services, retail finance, or enterprise corporate finance environments.·       Agile certifications such as SAFe, CSM, PSM, PMI-ACP.·       Experience supporting large-scale Agile programs or Release Train environments.BenefitsAbout Infoya:Infoya is a global IT solutions provider specializing in transforming complex challenges into streamlined, AI-powered outcomes. Through proprietary technology accelerators and full-scale enterprise services, Infoya automates workflows, enhances operational efficiency, and drives digital transformation across industries. With a presence in Canada, the US, India, and Costa Rica, we blend technical depth with creative problem-solving to deliver measurable impact. At Infoya, you’ll have the opportunity to work with a diverse and talented team in a dynamic and collaborative environment, with hybrid work options and strong support for professional development and growth. Salary Range: $95,000 to $105,000 CAD/ year The final compensation offered will depend on local market conditions and geographic location, as well as job-related factors such as the candidate’s knowledge, skills, qualifications, relevant experience, and education/training. Compensation may also include additional components such as benefits, and/or other incentives, where applicable. In accordance with new employment standards requirements, we retain copies of this job posting and applicant information for three (3) years after the posting is removed. We do not use AI technology; all applications are also reviewed by our recruitment team.Infoya is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veteran status, aboriginal status, or any other legally protected factors.

title: Senior Project Manager/Scrum Master
description: About Apply DigitalApply Digital is a global experience transformation partner. We drive AI-powered change and measurable impact across complex, multi-brand ecosystems. Leveraging expertise that spans across the customer experience lifecycle from strategy, design to engineering and beyond, we enable our clients to modernize their organizations and maximize value for their business and customers.Our 750+ team members have helped transform global companies like Kraft Heinz, NFL, Moderna, Lululemon, Dropbox, Atlassian, A+E Networks, and The Very Group.Apply Digital was founded in 2016 in Vancouver, Canada. In the past nine years, we have grown to ten cities across North America, South America, the UK, Europe, and India.At Apply Digital, we believe in the “ One Team ” approach, where we operate within a ‘pod’ structure. Each pod brings together senior leadership, subject matter experts, and cross-functional skill sets, all working within a common tech and delivery framework. This structure is underpinned by well-oiled scrum and sprint cadences, keeping teams in step to release often and retrospectives to ensure we progress toward the desired outcomes.Wherever we work in the world, we envision Apply Digital as a safe, empowered, respectful and fun community for people, every single day. Together, we work to embody our SHAPE (smart, humble, active, positive, and excellent) values and make Apply Digital a space for our team to connect, grow, and support each other to make a difference.Visit our Careers page to learn how we can unlock your potential.LOCATION: Apply Digital is hybrid/remote friendly. The preferred candidate should be based either in Canada or Latin America, in working in hours that align to ET (Eastern Timezone) or PT (Pacific Timezone).Candidates located in Santiago (Chile), Vancouver (Canada), or Toronto (Canada) are able to work from one of our offices as remote/hybrid employees. Candidates located outside of these locations will be fully remote employees.THE ROLE:Apply Digital is seeking a Senior Project Manager/Scrum Master with deep expertise in Scrum and Agile frameworks to lead complex digital programs. This role centers on guiding multiple digital development teams through structured, iterative delivery, ensuring clarity, alignment, and momentum across projects. You will bring agency or consultancy experience, having successfully led digital transformations and flagship initiatives, while bridging business objectives with technical execution. The ideal candidate thrives under pressure, drives disciplined processes, and can translate strategic priorities into actionable plans for cross-functional teams.In this position, you will be the primary Scrum Leader for several development squads, using your knowledge of Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid Agile approaches to maximize team performance, delivery quality, and client impact. Your role combines tactical project oversight with strategic thinking, balancing day-to-day execution with long-term program goals. Strong communication, facilitation, and stakeholder management skills are essential for ensuring alignment across teams, translating complex discussions into clear actions, and maintaining transparency with clients and leadership.Candidates must be able to work standard Pacific Time (PT) hours, from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM to support client deliverables and team collaborationRESPONSIBILITIES:Serve as Scrum Master for multiple digital development teams, facilitating sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives to drive focus, transparency, and continuous improvement. Lead complex digital projects from initiation to delivery, translating client outcomes into clear plans while managing scope, timeline, and budget. Guide teams through Agile and Kanban practices, adapting methodologies to team needs while fostering efficiency and delivery excellence. Define and maintain project roadmaps, release plans, and capacity schedules, proactively identifying risks, dependencies, and bottlenecks. Act as the primary liaison between technical and business stakeholders, ensuring project priorities align with strategic objectives and business value. Oversee the release process across multiple platforms, coordinating deployments, resolving blockers, and maintaining QA and performance standards. Facilitate workshops and discovery sessions, including requirements gathering, design reviews, and feedback cycles, to create shared understanding and alignment across teams. Collaborate with analytics, QA, and technical teams to inform decisions with data, validate outcomes, and ensure project success against KPIs and performance metrics. Maintain accurate documentation, progress tracking, and reporting in project management tools, ensuring transparency for leadership and client stakeholders. Manage third-party vendors and partners, ensuring adherence to agreements, quality standards, and integration within the overall delivery framework. Foster a collaborative, high-performing culture by monitoring team morale, encouraging learning, and promoting Agile best practices and adoption of effective tools. REQUIREMENTS:6-10 years of experience leading complex, end-to-end digital programs in an agency, consultancy, or professional services environment, ideally for enterprise or global clients, with a proven record of delivering flagship digital experiences and high-profile platforms. Prior experience as a Project Manager or Scrum Lead supporting digital initiatives, demonstrating the ability to navigate technical complexity, high visibility, and elevated expectations for performance, reliability, and delivery. Must be able to work during Pacific Time (PT) hours, typically 9:00 AM–5:00 PM as required to align with client and team schedules. Strong foundation in digital project management, including ownership of timelines, budgets, scope, and delivery across digital applications, with the agility to adapt to evolving client and business needs. Experience coordinating delivery across Identity, eCommerce, and other platform teams within complex digital ecosystems. Demonstrated technical delivery management experience, with the ability to communicate fluently to align technical and business stakeholders. Experienced in facilitating cross-functional collaboration among technical, design, data, and business teams—aligning priorities, driving accountability, and ensuring smooth communication across stakeholders. Experience managing headless or composable platforms across eCommerce, CMS, loyalty, and personalization systems (e.g., CommerceTools, Contentful, Talon.One , Auth0, Segment, etc.). Deep understanding of Agile delivery frameworks (Scrum, Kanban, or hybrid), the software development lifecycle (SDLC), and continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) practices. Agile or Scrum Master certifications (CSM, PMI-ACP, or equivalent) are a plus. Strong, energetic leadership presence, able to motivate teams, influence decision-making, and maintain focus under pressure while demonstrating professionalism, empathy, and composure. Highly emotionally intelligent and adaptable, capable of reading team dynamics, anticipating challenges, and adjusting communication styles to maintain alignment and trust with clients and internal teams. Skilled in stakeholder management and facilitation, from sprint ceremonies to executive reviews, translating discussions into actionable delivery plans and measurable outcomes. Technically fluent, able to engage confidently on topics including AI, analytics, cloud architecture, and platform integrations, using insights to support data-informed decision-making. Adept at managing vendors and external partners, defining scope, budgets, and SLAs, and ensuring seamless integration within broader delivery efforts. Proficient in project and collaboration tools such as Jira, Confluence, Airtable, Notion, Smartsheet, and Google Suite, with hands-on experience managing Jira development boards preferred. Skilled in tracking and reporting KPIs and performance metrics, leveraging insights to drive continuous improvement in processes and delivery outcomes. Strong relationship builder, capable of engaging confidently with Director, VP, and C-suite stakeholders while balancing diplomacy with clarity and sound judgment. A flexible, resilient, and proactive leader with a positive, can-do attitude—committed to fostering team growth, collaboration, and a one-team culture. Demonstrated critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, taking initiative to resolve ambiguity and drive projects toward clear, impactful results. Experience working with CRM or marketing platforms such as HubSpot is a plus; candidates with a technical background (e.g., prior developer or engineering experience) are highly valued. LIFE AT APPLY DIGITALAt Apply Digital, people are at the core of everything we do . We value your time, safety, and health, and strive to build a work community that can help you thrive and grow. Here are a few benefits we offer to support you:Great projects:  Broaden your skills on a range of engaging projects with international brands that have a global impact.An inclusive and safe environment:  We’re truly committed to building a culture where you are celebrated and everyone feels welcome and safe.Learning opportunities:  We offer generous training budgets, including partner tech certifications, custom learning plans, workshops, mentorship, and peer support.Generous vacation policy:  Work-life balance is key to our team’s success, so we offer ample time away from work to promote overall well-being.Flexible work arrangements: We work in a variety of ways, from remote, to in-office, to a blend of both.Apply Digital is committed to building a culture where differences are celebrated and everyone feels welcome. That’s why we value equal opportunity and nurture an inclusive workplace where our individual differences are recognized and valued.For more information, visit the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) section of our website. If you have accommodation needs at this stage of the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible by emailing us at [email protected] .We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Associate Project Manager
description: Best Workplaces™ in Canada 2020 - 2025 | Best Workplaces™ with Most Trusted Executive Teams 2024, 2025 | Best Workplaces™ in Financial Services & Insurance 2020 - 2025 | Best Workplaces™ for Mental Wellness 2023 - 2025 | Best Workplaces™ for Giving Back 2022, 2024 | Best Workplaces™ for Inclusion 2021, 2024 | Best Workplaces™ for Women 2021 | Best Workplaces™ in Ontario™ 2020, 2021, 2024 | Best Workplaces™ for Professional Development 2025 | Best Workplaces™ for Young Talent 2026One of Canada’s Most Trusted Investment Brands.At Picton Mahoney Asset Management (PICTON Investments), being alternative isn’t just what we do—it’s who we are.Founded by industry pioneer David Picton, we’ve spent 20 years challenging conventional wisdom and redefining the investing landscape. As one of Canada’s most trusted investment brands, we are proudly independent—privately run, 100% employee-owned, and deeply committed to delivering results for our clients.Thinking Alternatively is in Our DNA.With a team of 207 bold thinkers—one-third dedicated solely to investment management—we are specialists, not generalists. Managing $16.6 billion (as of December 31, 2025) for institutional and retail clients, we navigate markets with conviction, resilience, and a forward-thinking approach.We’re more than investors — we’re innovators. We challenge traditional investment mindsets, constantly pushing the boundaries to achieve our mission: To bring greater certainty to investors.Our success is built on four guiding principles:Treat investors’ money like our own.Redefine the way investors invest.Be human—always approachable.Succeed together, one investment at a time.These aren’t just words—they define how we think, how we invest, and how we work.Now, we’re entering our next chapter. We’re transforming our brand and reshaping the way the world invests. If you think alternatively, embrace an entrepreneurial spirit, and thrive in a dynamic, bureaucracy-free environment, join us and be part of what’s next.Thriving in Our Entrepreneurial CultureAt PICTON Investments, you’ll thrive in our creative and dynamic workplace, where collaboration and support are at the core of everything we do. At our firm, you’ll have the opportunity to take on significant responsibilities, work in a flexible environment, and tackle challenging projects from the outset. Our culture is designed for ambitious professionals who want to make an immediate impact while continuing to push boundaries and achieve our greater mission. You’ll be empowered with a high level of responsibility, trust, and flexibility, providing an exciting and creative space for you to help reshape how the world views alternative investments. With competitive total rewards, performance-based bonuses, and a clear path for career growth, you’ll have everything you need to develop both now and over the longer term.The OpportunityWe are seeking a high-energy, detail-oriented Associate Project Manager to support our Strategy and Delivery team. Reporting to the Project Manager, this role will assist in the planning, coordination, and execution of strategic initiatives across the organization.This position is ideal for someone with 3–5 years of project coordination or junior project management experience, preferably within financial services, who is eager to grow their career and gain exposure to enterprise-level initiatives, new business launches, and cross-functional collaboration.The Associate Project Manager will work closely with senior project managers, business leaders, and external partners to ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope, and to a high standard, while aligning with business objectives.This role requires someone who can stay composed and effective in fast‑changing environments and who feels comfortable navigating ambiguity. The Associate Project Manager should be able to adjust plans quickly, respond positively to shifting priorities, maintain momentum and help remove roadblocks when unexpected challenges arise.The expected base salary for this role is $75,000 – $95,000, with the final number depending on your experience, skills, education, and team budget. On top of that, you’ll be eligible for our discretionary bonus program.At PICTON, being employee-owned means, we take care of our team and offer opportunities for top performers to participate in our equity ownership program over time. Eligible employees get access to a full benefits package, including profit sharing, health and wellness coverage, life and disability insurance, paid time off and holidays, learning and development programs, and more, many of which are fully covered or subsidized by us.Responsibilities of the RoleSupport the delivery of strategic projects by assisting with: Project plans, timelines, milestones, and deliverables, scope tracking and change request documentation & resource coordination and task trackingAssist in gathering, documenting, and communicating business and technical requirements in alignment with compliance, legal, and operational frameworks.Coordinate with internal teams (e.g., Business, Operations, Finance, IT, Compliance) to track progress against project milestones.Maintain project documentation including status reports, meeting notes, risk logs, and action items.Track risks, issues, and dependencies, escalating concerns to senior project managers as appropriate.Support vendor and third-party coordination, including meeting scheduling, deliverable tracking, and follow-ups.Assist with project communications to stakeholders, including preparing materials for leadership updates.Participate in project team meetings and cross-functional forums, representing the project as needed.Contribute to post-project reviews and retrospectives to capture lessons learned and improve future delivery.Support initiatives related to business operations, process improvements, and new product or service launches.What we’re looking for3–5 years of experience in project coordination, junior project management, or a related role.Experience in asset management, wealth management, or investment business is strongly preferred. Candidates with backgrounds in general financial services or other compliance‑driven, regulated environments are also encouraged to apply.Experience working on a variety of projects, ranging from business initiatives to system‑related projects, as well as supporting cross-functional teams and managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.Strong organizational and communication skills, with attention to detail.Working knowledge of project management fundamentals (planning, scheduling, risk tracking, reporting).Proficiency with common tools such as Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook), Teams, and SharePointExperienced in using project management tools and preparing reports for various audiences to support effective decision‑making and keep the team aligned with project goals.Demonstrating the ability to identify issues, analyze root causes, and propose practical solutions that keep projects moving forward will be considered a strong asset.Bachelor’s degree in Business, Project Management, Finance, or a related field.Exposure to Agile or hybrid delivery methodologies is an asset.PMP, CAPM, Scrum, or other project certifications are nice to have but not required.Our Commitment to EmployeesAt PICTON Investments, we take pride in enhancing our employees’ experiences through a comprehensive suite of exceptional perks and programs. Our benefits include corporate fitness reimbursement and discounts, VersaFi memberships (formerly Women in Capital Markets), Picton investment counseling and portfolio management services, volunteer and charitable donation matching, maternity and parental leave top-up, recognition awards, semi-annual performance bonuses, a generous annual vacation entitlement (minimum of 15 days per year), training and development reimbursement, extensive health and dental benefits, a healthcare spending account, and more.These offerings are designed to support your career growth, well-being, and overall success. Join us and thrive in an environment that values and invests in you!PICTON Investments is committed to providing an equitable and fair work environment for everyone and all hiring and other personnel actions will be taken without regard to race, colour, creed, religion, sex, disability, gender identity, gender expression, family status, age, language or national origin. We welcome applications from candidates with diverse experiences globally. Canadian experience is not required. If you require an accommodation at any point in time throughout the application and hiring process, please contact Human Resources at (416) 955-4108 or at accessibility@pictoninvestments.com.PICTON Investments does not accept unsolicited resumes, emails, calls, or any other form of communication from third-party recruitment agencies. Any unsolicited outreach, including commercial electronic messages, will neither be acknowledged nor considered.Please note: We use AI-assisted tools to support parts of our recruitment process, including summarizing interview notes and aligning candidate profiles with job requirements. All decisions are made by our hiring team.

title: Senior Project Manager
description: Who We Are:Every transaction matters. Every Canadian matters. At Interac, we protect both — driving trust, security, and inclusion, so our digital economy thrives.Founded in 1984, Interac connects Canadians through secure digital payments, advanced identity verification and industry-leading fraud protection. Connecting banks, businesses, and individuals, Interac enables millions to send, receive, and manage money safely and effortlessly every day — across both digital and physical environments.As the backbone of Canada’s financial ecosystem, Interac facilitates over 20 million transactions daily, supported by trusted partnerships with government and financial institutions. Consistently ranked as Canada’s most reputable financial technology brand, Interac is deeply embedded in the daily lives of Canadians.Who You Will Work With:The vacant Senior Project Manager role will report to the Leader, Agile Practice. We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Project Manager to oversee the successful execution and delivery of complex workstreams/initiatives/projects/capabilities within our organization.What You Will Do:Hands-on planning and delivery of product (business) and technology initiatives that span the domains of product development, software development, infrastructure and security. Leading the development of integrated delivery plans and driving the successful orchestration of dependencies and prioritization with delivery squads. Actively engaging with Product Owners, Service Owners, domain leads, executive leaders, and external partners to enable effective and impactful decisions, facilitate coordination and alignment across various stakeholders, and to facilitate prioritization of initiatives, epics, and stories within the corresponding backlogs. Partnering with key leaders, the Enterprise Project Management Office, the Agile Practice, and working teams across the organization to provide insights and visibility into delivery progress, risks, issues, and metrics. Proactively identify risks, designing and executing mitigation strategies to avoid issues, and effectively escalating as needed. Enabling achievement of initiatives within and across working teams through effective change management practices (scope, timeline, financials), clearly communicating objectives and coordinating milestone check-ins. Using personal influence and encouraging the use of Agile and delivery mindset practices and tools to accelerate change. Identifying opportunities to streamline efforts and refine processes. What You Bring:Delivery Leadership: Contribute to the planning of initiatives, and responsible for driving the planning and execution of epics and stories, ensuring alignment with delivery frameworks. Dependency Management: Accountable for proactively identifying, tracking, and resolving internal and external dependencies and risks to delivery. This includes managing cross-team dependencies (between various squads or various Product Owners), vendor relationships, regulatory requirements, identifying and communicating resource constraints. Delivery Management: Responsible for overseeing the delivery of epics and stories, facilitating the prioritization and sequencing of work with contributing squads and teams. Product Alignment: Engage with Product Owners and Service Owners to align sprint, quarterly, and annual plans with identified dependencies and delivery activities. Stakeholder Management: Communicate effectively with stakeholders at all levels, providing regular updates and managing expectations. This includes proactively communicating dependency impacts and negotiating solutions across squads/products as appropriate Team Collaboration: Facilitate cross-functional collaboration between teams, resolving dependencies and impediments to ensure seamless delivery. Deep understanding of Agile principles and practices. Strong leadership, communication, problem-solving, and negotiation skills. Experience with dependency management tools and techniques. Experience with project management tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence). Relevant certifications (e.g., PMI-ACP, SAFe Agilist, PMP) are a plus. Eligibility to work for Interac Corp. in Canada in a full-time capacity. What We’re Offering:The hiring range for this position is $120,000 - $130,000, and you will also be eligible for our short-term incentive plan. The exact amount will depend on factors such as skills, experience, and job-related knowledge, but Interac’s commitment goes beyond compensation. Our Total Rewards package is designed to support your well-being and future, and includes:Generous vacation and wellness days to help you recharge Comprehensive employer-paid benefits coverage for peace of mind Market-leading employer-funded RRSP program to invest in your future Flexible hybrid work model for better work-life balance Access to a free and confidential 24/7 employee & family assistance program to offer support for you and your immediate family Pregnancy and parental leave top-up to support growing families Charitable donation matching with United Way to amplify your impactWhy Join Us?At Interac, the impact we make, and the people who drive it, is profound. When you become part of our team, you’re joining a purpose-driven organization that’s shaping the future of digital finance in Canada. Here’s what you can expect:Investing in the Future – Help us unlock digital prosperity for all Canadians. Innovative Thinking – Collaborate on products, practices, and platforms that redefine what’s possible. Inclusive Culture – Be empowered to bring your whole self to work and realize your full potential. Inspiring Community – Work in an ecosystem where we lift each other up and rise together. Intentional Support – Enjoy flexible, supportive offerings that prioritize your total wellness. Additional Pre-Employment Requirements:To ensure the integrity of our organization, successful candidates will be required to complete background checks, which may include, Canadian Criminal Credit Check, Canadian ID Cross-Check, Public Safety Verification, 5-year Employment Verification, Education Verification, Credit Check, and Social Media Check.Equal Opportunity EmployerInterac is also an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe that innovation thrives when people from different backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives come together. That’s why we are committed to providing fair and equitable employment opportunities for all individuals, without discrimination based on race, color, ancestry, ethnic origin, place of origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, marital or family status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.If you require accommodation during any stage of the application or recruitment process, please contact us at humanresources@interac.ca. We will work with you to meet your needs.Please be aware that certain individuals are misusing Interac Corp.’s name and logo to promote fictitious employment opportunities. Interac Corp. never requests, solicits, or accepts any form of payment in exchange for employment. Any such offers are fraudulent and should be disregarded. Interac Corp. assumes no liability for any claims, losses, damages, expenses, or inconveniences arising from or related to these fraudulent activities. Such communications do not constitute an offer or representation by Interac Corp. or its subsidiaries and affiliates.

title: Senior Project Manager

title: Senior Project Manager / Scrum Master - GFT
description: Job DescriptionWhat Is The Opportunity?Are you a talented, creative, and results-driven professional who thrives on delivering high-performing applications? title: Construction Project Manager (Residential)
description: À propos du posteNotre client, un chef de file dans le domaine de la rénovation résidentielle haut de gamme, est à la recherche d'un gestionnaire de projet de construction expérimenté pour diriger et superviser des projets de construction résidentielle et commerciale à Montréal et dans les régions avoisinantes. Le candidat idéal est un professionnel organisé, soucieux du détail, qui possède une connaissance approfondie de la construction de maisons de luxe et qui a fait ses preuves dans la réalisation de projets complexes dans le respect des délais, du budget et des normes de qualité les plus élevées.Principales responsabilitésPlanifier, coordonner et gérer toutes les phases des projets de construction résidentielle haut de gamme, de la pré-construction à l'achèvement.Assurer la liaison avec les clients, les architectes, les concepteurs, les sous-traitants et les fournisseurs afin de garantir une communication claire et un alignement tout au long du cycle de vie du projet.Créer et tenir à jour des calendriers, des budgets et des rapports de coûts détaillés pour les projets.Veiller à ce que les travaux soient exécutés conformément aux codes du bâtiment, aux règles de sécurité et aux normes de l'entreprise.Effectuer des visites de chantier, superviser l'avancement des travaux et résoudre rapidement tout problème sur le chantierGérer et négocier les contrats avec les sous-traitants et les fournisseursRendre compte régulièrement à la direction et aux clients de l'état d'avancement du projet, des délais et du budgetMaintenir un niveau élevé de satisfaction de la clientèle grâce à un service et une communication excellentsQualificationsPlus de 5 ans d'expérience dans la gestion de projets de construction résidentielle haut de gammeSolide connaissance des méthodes de construction, des matériaux et des codes du bâtiment.Maîtrise du français (parlé et écrit) ; de solides compétences en anglais sont essentielles.Solides compétences en matière de leadership, d'organisation et de résolution de problèmesCompétences avérées en matière de leadership et de gestionMaîtrise de Procore ou BuildertrendCapacité à lire et à interpréter des dessins d'architecture et d'ingénieriePermis de conduire valide et volonté de se déplacer sur les chantiers de la région de MontréalDEC ou baccalauréat en gestion de la construction, en architecture ou dans un domaine connexe est un atout.Ce qu'ils offrentSalaire concurrentiel basé sur l'expériencePlan de jumelage REERRégime d'assurance maladie completVéhicule de fonctionPossibilités de croissance professionnelle dans un environnement de haut calibre axé sur la clientèleAbout the RoleOur client, a leader in high-end residential renovations, is seeking an experienced Construction Project Manager to lead and oversee residential and commercial construction projects in Montreal and surrounding areas. The ideal candidate is an organized, detail-oriented professional with a deep understanding of luxury home construction and a proven track record of delivering complex projects on time, within budget, and to the highest quality standards.Key ResponsibilitiesPlan, coordinate, and manage all phases of high-end residential construction projects from pre-construction to completionLiaise with clients, architects, designers, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure clear communication and alignment throughout the project lifecycleCreate and maintain detailed project schedules, budgets, and cost reportsEnsure work is performed in compliance with building codes, safety regulations, and company standardsConduct site visits, oversee site progress, and resolve any on-site issues promptlyManage and negotiate contracts with subcontractors and vendorsReport regularly to senior management and clients on project status, timelines, and budgetMaintain a high level of customer satisfaction through excellent service and communicationQualifications5+ years of experience managing high-end residential construction projectsStrong knowledge of construction methods, materials, and building codes relevantFluent in French (spoken and written); strong English communication skills essentialStrong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skillsProven leadership and managerial skillsProficient in Procore or BuilderTrendAbility to read and interpret architectural and engineering drawingsValid driver's license and willingness to travel to job sites around MontrealDEC or bachelor’s degree in construction management, architecture, or a related field is an assetWhat They OfferCompetitive salary based on experienceRRSP Matching planComprehensive health insurance planCompany vehicleOpportunities for professional growth in a high-caliber, client-focused environment

title: Senior Project Manager/Product Owner (Hybrid)
description: We’re seeking someone to join our team as a Senior Project Manager/Product Owner to lead cross-functional AI initiatives with a primary focus on enterprise deployments of AI products like ChatGPT and copilot and adjacent generative AI capabilities.You will drive complex, multi-region programs from discovery through rollout and adoption, ensuring delivery quality, security, compliance, and measurable business value. This role sits at the intersection of program leadership, AI platform operations, and stakeholder management across technology and business teams.In the Technology division, we leverage innovation to build the connections and capabilities that power our Firm, enabling our clients and colleagues to redefine markets and shape the future of our communities. This is a Lead Cloud & Infrastructure Engineering position at Vice President level, which is part of the job family responsible for managing and optimizing technical infrastructure and ensuring the seamless operation of IT systems to support business needs effectively.Since 1935, Morgan Stanley is known as a global leader in financial services, always evolving and innovating to better serve our clients and our communities in more than 40 countries around the world.Interested in joining a team that’s eager to create, innovate and make an impact on the world? Read on…What You’ll Do In The RoleOwn end-to-end delivery of AI projects from planning and governance through execution, hypercare, and handover to operations. Establish and manage integrated plans, RAID logs, decision records, scope baselines, and executive reporting for multi-workstream programs.Coordinate configuration, deployment, and scaling like ChatGPT or copilot in alignment with security, compliance, and regulatory requirements (e.g., DLP, access management/SSO/SCIM, audit logging, responsible AI policies).Partner with platform engineering to enable automation, environment readiness, and integrations (e.g., M365, ServiceNow, Slack, Azure OpenAI, Entra ID) and to track operational SLAs/OKRs.Lead risk assessments and design reviews for AI solutions; ensure adherence to governance, data privacy and model usage guidelines.Plan and orchestrate testing and quality assurance activities (manual and automated), UAT, performance validation, and change management for enterprise-wide rollouts.Facilitate cross-functional collaboration among security, legal, data protection, architecture, engineering, and business product owners; manage vendor relationships and escalations.Define adoption and enablement plans, training, communications, and success metrics; monitor usage, performance, and cost efficiency and drive continuous improvement.Drive incident response, problem management, and post-implementation reviews to ensure platform reliability and user satisfaction.What You’ll Bring To The RoleBSc/BA in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field (or equivalent experience). PMP and/or Scrum certification is an asset.8+ years of technology project/program management in enterprise environments; proven delivery across multi-country or regulated industries (financial services preferred).Experience of Connectors and or Power platform is an assetDirect experience leading deployments of AI products across large global Enterprises or similar LLM-based solutions; strong understanding of generative AI concepts and enterprise integration patterns.Solid grasp of AI governance and risk frameworks; experience implementing security and compliance controls (DLP/Purview, audit logging, access management, encryption).Hands-on familiarity with automation and scripting practices (e.g., Python, PowerShell) and CI/CD concepts to coordinate with engineering and QA.Expert stakeholder management and communication skills; ability to articulate complex technical topics to non-technical audiences and senior executives.Fluency managing hybrid delivery models (Agile, Scrum, and phase-gated); proficiency with Jira/Confluence, Project/Planner, and ServiceNow for demand and incident processes.Bilingual (English/French) strongly preferred for Montreal-based stakeholders and partners.At Morgan Stanley Montreal, we support the Firm’s global businesses and infrastructure with cutting edge technology and innovation. The multi-faceted and highly technical Montreal team plays a critical role in building and maintaining our leading technology platform, including electronic trading, algorithm trading, cloud engineering, infrastructure, cybersecurity and AI/ML. Morgan Stanley has been rooted in the Montreal community since 2008 and is considered a leading employer among the area’s highly skilled technology talent. There’s ample opportunity to move across the businesses for those who show passion and grit in their work.All our positions are located in Montreal, Quebec. We offer a hybrid work environment, combining remote work and attendance in the office.Knowledge of French and English is required.Build a career with impact. Visit morganstanley.com for more information.What You Can Expect From Morgan StanleyAt Morgan Stanley, we raise, manage and allocate capital for our clients – helping them reach their goals. We do it in a way that’s differentiated – and we’ve done that for 90 years. Our values - putting clients first, doing the right thing, leading with exceptional ideas, committing to diversity and inclusion, and giving back - aren’t just beliefs, they guide the decisions we make every day to do what's best for our clients, communities and more than 80,000 employees in 1,200 offices across 42 countries. At Morgan Stanley, you’ll find an opportunity to work alongside the best and the brightest, in an environment where you are supported and empowered. Our teams are relentless collaborators and creative thinkers, fueled by their diverse backgrounds and experiences. We are proud to support our employees and their families at every point along their work-life journey, offering some of the most attractive and comprehensive employee benefits and perks in the industry. There’s also ample opportunity to move about the business for those who show passion and grit in their work.To learn more about our offices across the globe, please copy and paste https://www.morganstanley.com/about-us/global-offices into your browser.Morgan Stanley is an equal opportunities employer. We work to provide a supportive and inclusive environment where all individuals can maximize their full potential.
